2026-03-18T16:02:25.0599684Z [36;1mSTOP_TOKEN=$(cat /proc/sys/kernel/random/uuid)[0m
2026-03-18T16:02:25.0600287Z [36;1m[0m
2026-03-18T16:02:25.0600832Z [36;1m# Use a trap to ensure we always resume command processing and check for[0m
2026-03-18T16:02:25.0601795Z [36;1m# fallback error annotations, even if the runtime exits with a non-zero code[0m
2026-03-18T16:02:25.0602783Z [36;1m# (which would otherwise cause set -e to abort the shell before we get here).[0m
2026-03-18T16:02:25.0603668Z [36;1m# The trap preserves the original exit code.[0m
2026-03-18T16:02:25.0604209Z [36;1mcopilot_cleanup() {[0m
2026-03-18T16:02:25.0604743Z [36;1m  echo "::$STOP_TOKEN::"[0m
2026-03-18T16:02:25.0605642Z [36;1m  FALLBACK_FILE="${RUNNER_TEMP}/copilot-fallback-error.txt"[0m
2026-03-18T16:02:25.0606356Z [36;1m  if [ -f "$FALLBACK_FILE" ]; then[0m
2026-03-18T16:02:25.0606996Z [36;1m    FALLBACK_MSG=$(head -c 500 "$FALLBACK_FILE" | tr -d '\n\r')[0m
2026-03-18T16:02:25.0607874Z [36;1m    echo "::error title=Copilot Error::${FALLBACK_MSG}"[0m
2026-03-18T16:02:25.0608452Z [36;1m    rm -f "$FALLBACK_FILE"[0m
2026-03-18T16:02:25.0608900Z [36;1m  fi[0m
2026-03-18T16:02:25.0609281Z [36;1m}[0m

2026-03-18T16:02:25.9697552Z     url: { scheme: ["https"], domain: static.crates.io }
2026-03-18T16:02:25.9698094Z   - kind: http-rule
2026-03-18T16:02:25.9698533Z     url: { scheme: ["https"], domain: sh.rustup.rs }
2026-03-18T16:02:25.9699278Z   - kind: http-rule
2026-03-18T16:02:25.9699748Z     url: { scheme: ["https"], domain: static.rust-lang.org }
2026-03-18T16:02:25.9700179Z

2026-03-18T16:02:25.9701264Z   - kind: http-rule

2026-03-18T16:02:28.1123395Z Start agent firewall / Agent firewall started
2026-03-18T16:02:29.9537761Z (node:4628) ExperimentalWarning: SQLite is an experimental feature and might change at any time
2026-03-18T16:02:29.9538949Z (Use `node --trace-warnings ...` to show where the warning was created)
2026-03-18T16:02:30.5643763Z ::add-mask::2ff8549c0bbf2a49d97c99b7f829f870e7648c3159d37e7c31ace68b3d75cc2c
2026-03-18T16:02:30.5645951Z ::add-mask::2ff8549c0bbf2a49d97c99b7f829f870e7648c3159d37e7c31ace68b3d75cc2c
2026-03-18T16:02:30.5699890Z Reading job config for job ID 1397711-622110847-27af57a1-f239-4c71-a925-d75024b53650.
2026-03-18T16:02:31.1746597Z Proceeding without custom agent.
2026-03-18T16:02:31.7354064Z Responding to feedback: 1397711-622110847-27af57a1-f239-4c71-a925-d75024b53650 from GregorGullwi/FlashCpp@copilot/fix-missing-adl-features
2026-03-18T16:02:31.7364028Z Problem statement:
2026-03-18T16:02:31.7364282Z 
2026-03-18T16:02:31.7364408Z ----
2026-03-18T16:02:31.7365434Z *This section includes details on the pull request with code changes you have already submitted to fix the problem above.*
2026-03-18T16:02:31.7366327Z 
2026-03-18T16:02:31.7366458Z <pr_title>
2026-03-18T16:02:31.7366887Z Fix ADL for nested enum types and free-function operators
2026-03-18T16:02:31.7367430Z </pr_title>
2026-03-18T16:02:31.7367607Z 
2026-03-18T16:02:31.7367735Z <pr_description>
2026-03-18T16:02:31.7368611Z `test_hidden_friend_enum_adl_ret0.cpp` fails because two ADL features documented in `MISSING_FEATURES.md` were unimplemented.
2026-03-18T16:02:31.7369481Z 
2026-03-18T16:02:31.7369654Z ### Nested enum type resolution for ADL
2026-03-18T16:02:31.7369988Z 
2026-03-18T16:02:31.7378892Z Enums declared inside class bodies were registered in `gTypesByName` with only their simple name (`"Status"`). When used as a qualified type in function parameters, `lookupTypeInCurrentContext("Container::Status")` failed—producing `Type::UserDefined` with `type_index=0` instead of the correct `Type::Enum`. Overload resolution then rejected every ADL candidate.
2026-03-18T16:02:31.7381392Z 
2026-03-18T16:02:31.7383384Z Fix: walk `struct_parsing_context_stack_` to build the full struct chain at any depth, then use `gNamespaceRegistry.buildQualifiedIdentifier(current_namespace_handle, ...)` to prepend the namespace. This registers both the struct-relative name and the namespace-fully-qualified name, handling all nesting depths:
2026-03-18T16:02:31.7385733Z 
2026-03-18T16:02:31.7385862Z ```cpp
2026-03-18T16:02:31.7386405Z // Parser_Decl_StructEnum.cpp — after addNestedEnumIndex()
2026-03-18T16:02:31.7387008Z StringBuilder struct_chain_builder;
2026-03-18T16:02:31.7387564Z for (const auto& ctx : struct_parsing_context_stack_) {
2026-03-18T16:02:31.7388245Z     struct_chain_builder.append(ctx.struct_name).append("::");
2026-03-18T16:02:31.7388810Z }
2026-03-18T16:02:31.7389159Z struct_chain_builder.append(enum_decl.name());
2026-03-18T16:02:31.7390121Z StringHandle struct_relative_handle = StringTable::getOrInternStringHandle(struct_chain_builder.commit());
2026-03-18T16:02:31.7391203Z gTypesByName.emplace(struct_relative_handle, enum_it->second);
2026-03-18T16:02:31.7392108Z if (!qualified_namespace.empty()) {
2026-03-18T16:02:31.7392885Z     StringHandle ns_qualified_handle = gNamespaceRegistry.buildQualifiedIdentifier(
2026-03-18T16:02:31.7393744Z         current_namespace_handle, struct_relative_handle);
2026-03-18T16:02:31.7394426Z     gTypesByName.emplace(ns_qualified_handle, enum_it->second);
2026-03-18T16:02:31.7394997Z }
2026-03-18T16:02:31.7395444Z ```
2026-03-18T16:02:31.7395600Z 
2026-03-18T16:02:31.7395738Z This correctly handles:
2026-03-18T16:02:31.7396248Z - **Single-level struct in namespace**: `ns::Container::Status`
2026-03-18T16:02:31.7396899Z - **Multi-level struct nesting**: `ns::A::B::C::E`
2026-03-18T16:02:31.7397594Z - **Multi-level namespace nesting**: `outer::inner::Container::Status`
2026-03-18T16:02:31.7398104Z 
2026-03-18T16:02:31.7398331Z ### Operator ADL for regular free-function operators
2026-03-18T16:02:31.7398719Z 
2026-03-18T16:02:31.7400115Z `findBinaryOperatorOverloadWithFreeFunction` called `lookup_adl_only()` (hidden friends only). Regular free-function operators in associated namespaces were invisible to operator resolution.
2026-03-18T16:02:31.7401496Z 
2026-03-18T16:02:31.7402579Z Fix: replace with `lookup_adl()`, deduplicate against `lookup_all()` results using mangled names (`std::string_view` into stable interned storage) for semantically-correct identity comparison.
2026-03-18T16:02:31.7403847Z 
2026-03-18T16:02:31.7403976Z ### Tests & docs
2026-03-18T16:02:31.7404175Z 
2026-03-18T16:02:31.7404399Z - New test: `test_operator_adl_free_function_ret0.cpp`
2026-03-18T16:02:31.7405294Z - New test: `test_nested_struct_enum_adl_ret0.cpp` (3-level struct nesting ADL)
2026-03-18T16:02:31.7406083Z - `MISSING_FEATURES.md`: both ADL entries marked FIXED
2026-03-18T16:02:31.7406671Z - Full suite: 1576 pass / 0 fail / 56 expected-fail
2026-03-18T16:02:31.7407174Z </pr_description>
2026-03-18T16:02:31.7407375Z 
2026-03-18T16:02:31.7407614Z ## Comments on the PR (you are @copilot in this section)
2026-03-18T16:02:31.7408027Z 
2026-03-18T16:02:31.7408150Z <comments>
2026-03-18T16:02:31.7408325Z

2026-03-18T16:02:31.7417842Z

2026-03-18T16:02:31.7418262Z 
2026-03-18T16:02:31.7420032Z The nested enum registration now uses `struct_parsing_context_stack_` (which already includes all nesting levels) to build the full struct chain, then appends the namespace prefix via `gNamespaceRegistry.buildQualifiedIdentifier(current_namespace_handle, ...)`:
2026-03-18T16:02:31.7421970Z 
2026-03-18T16:02:31.7423386Z - **Multi-level struct nesting**: `ns::A::B::C::E` — walks the full context stack (`A`, `B`, `C`) to build `"A::B::C::E"`, then prepends the namespace to get `"ns::A::B::C::E"`. New test: `test_nested_struct_enum_adl_ret0.cpp`.
2026-03-18T16:02:31.7426357Z - **Multi-level namespace nesting**: `outer::inner::Container::Status` — `buildQualifiedIdentifier` uses the stored `NamespaceHandle` (which carries the full `"outer::inner"` qualified name), so this was already correct.
2026-03-18T16:02:31.7429246Z - **Operator ADL dedup**: switched to mangled-name–based deduplication (`std::string_view` into stable interned storage) as Gemini's review suggested.
2026-03-18T16:02:31.7430270Z

,"avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/811515?v=4"},"commit_id":"98f3679471a72f3d4c5b8a3852c9fd140a2f61d6","submitted_at":"2026-03-18T16:00:29Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR"},{"id":3968997731,"state":"COMMENTED","html_url":"https://github.com/GregorGullwi/FlashCpp/pull/943#pullrequestreview-3968997731","user":{"login":"devin-ai-integration[bot]","id":158243242,"profile_url":"https://github.com/apps/devin-ai-integration","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/811515?v=4"},"commit_id":"98f3679471a72f3d4c5b8a3852c9fd140a2f61d6","submitted_at":"2026-03-18T16:00:48Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR"}]
2026-03-18T16:02:45.3770227Z 
2026-03-18T16:02:45.3770791Z function:
2026-03-18T16:02:45.3771240Z   name: github-mcp-server-pull_request_read
2026-03-18T16:02:45.3771847Z   args:
2026-03-18T16:02:45.3772203Z     method: get_review_comments
2026-03-18T16:02:45.3772639Z     owner: GregorGullwi
2026-03-18T16:02:45.3773035Z     pullNumber: 943
2026-03-18T16:02:45.3773386Z     repo: FlashCpp
2026-03-18T16:02:45.3828832Z   result: {"review_threads":[{"is_resolved":true,"is_outdated":true,"is_collapsed":true,"comments":[{"body":"![critical](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/critical.svg)\n\nThis deduplication logic is incorrect. It compares pointers to `FunctionDeclarationNode`s, but `lookup_all` and `lookup_adl` return copies of `ASTNode`s, so pointer comparison does not work for identifying unique functions.\n\nAdditionally, `overloads.push_back()` can reallocate the vector, invalidating the pointers in the `existing` set and causing a use-after-free bug.\n\nA better approach is to deduplicate using a unique identifier like the function's mangled name.\n\n```c\n\t\t// Build a set of existing function mangled names for O(1) dedup.\n\t\tstd::unordered_set<std::string_view> existing_mangled_names;\n\t\texisting_mangled_names.reserve(overloads.size());\n\t\tfor (const auto& node : overloads) {\n\t\t\tif (node.is<FunctionDeclarationNode>()) {\n\t\t\t\tconst auto& func_decl = node.as<FunctionDeclarationNode>();\n\t\t\t\tif (func_decl.has_mangled_name()) {\n\t\t\t\t\texisting_mangled_names.insert(func_decl.mangled_name());\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t\tfor (auto& cand : adl_candidates) {\n\t\t\tif (cand.is<FunctionDeclarationNode>()) {\n\t\t\t\tconst auto& func_decl = cand.as<FunctionDeclarationNode>();\n\t\t\t\t// If the function has no mangled name, we cannot reliably deduplicate it, so we add it.\n\t\t\t\t// Otherwise, we only add it if its mangled name is not already present.\n\t\t\t\tif (!func_decl.has_mangled_name() || existing_mangled_names.insert(func_decl.mangled_name()).second) {\n\t\t\t\t\toverloads.push_back(std::move(cand));\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\t// Non-function nodes are not deduplicated for now.\n\t\t\t\toverloads.push_back(std::move(cand));\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n```","path":"src/OverloadResolution.h","author":"gemini-code-assist","created_at":"2026-03-18T15:08:55Z","updated_at":"2026-03-18T15:08:55Z","html_url":"https://github.com/GregorGullwi/FlashCpp/pull/943#discussion_r2954140128"}],"total_count":1},{"is_resolved":true,"is_outdated":false,"is_collapsed":true,"comments":[{"body":"![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)\n\nThe date `2026-03-18` is used for both fixed issues, but this date is in the future. This appears to be a typo.","path":"docs/MISSING_FEATURES.md","line":425,"author":"gemini-code-assist","created_at":"2026-03-18T15:08:55Z","updated_at":"2026-03-18T15:08:55Z","html_url":"https://github.com/GregorGullwi/FlashCpp/pull/943#discussion_r2954140141"}],"total_count":1},{"is_resolved":true,"is_outdated":true,"is_collapsed":true,"comments":[{"body":"<!-- devin-review-comment {\"id\": \"ANALYSIS_pr-review-job-c36cf1119db2427db42ea0011bfd664f_0003\", \"file_path\": \"src/Parser_Decl_StructEnum.cpp\", \"start_line\": 757, \"end_line\": 767, \"side\": \"RIGHT\"} -->\n\n🚩 **Nested enum qualified name registration only covers one nesting level**\n\nThe new code at `src/Parser_Decl_StructEnum.cpp:757-767` registers the nested enum as `type_name + \"::\" + enum_decl.name()`. For a struct `ns::Container` with enum `Status`, this produces `ns::Container::Status`. However, `type_name` is constructed at lines 88-115 and for nested classes it uses only one level of parent (e.g., `Outer::Inner` but not `ns::Outer::Inner` if `Outer` is itself in namespace `ns` AND a nested class). This is a pre-existing limitation of `type_name` construction for deeply nested classes within namespaces, not introduced by this PR. For the stated fix target (enum inside a class in a namespace), it works correctly because `type_name` at line 115 is set to the fully namespace-qualified name.\n\n<!-- devin-review-badge-begin -->\n<a href=\"https://app.devin.ai/review/gregorgullwi/flashcpp/pull/943\" target=\"_blank\">\n  <picture>\n    <source media=\"(prefers-color-scheme: dark)\" srcset=\"https://static.devin.ai/assets/gh-open-in-devin-review-dark.svg?v=1\">\n    <img src=\"https://static.devin.ai/assets/gh-open-in-devin-review-light.svg?v=1\" alt=\"Open in Devin Review\">\n  </picture>\n</a>\n<!-- devin-review-badge-end -->\n\n---\n*Was this helpful? React with 👍 or 👎 to provide feedback.*","path":"src/Parser_Decl_StructEnum.cpp","author":"devin-ai-integration","created_at":"2026-03-18T15:30:08Z","updated_at":"2026-03-18T15:30:09Z","html_url":"https://github.com/GregorGullwi/FlashCpp/pull/943#discussion_r2954316509"},{"body":"✅ **Resolved**: The code has been completely rewritten to walk struct_parsing_context_stack_ from outermost to innermost (lines 771-776), correctly handling arbitrary nesting depth (e.g., A::B::C::E). The previous concern about only covering one nesting level is no longer applicable.","path":"src/Parser_Decl_StructEnum.cpp","author":"devin-ai-integration","created_at":"2026-03-18T15:54:24Z","updated_at":"2026-03-18T15:54:25Z","html_url":"https://github.com/GregorGullwi/FlashCpp/pull/943#discussion_r2954481343"}],"total_count":2},{"is_resolved":false,"is_outdated":false,"is_collapsed":false,"comments":[{"body":"<!-- devin-review-comment {\"id\": \"ANALYSIS_pr-review-job-8da89a4ee66e4231bc21ac43eba03936_0002\", \"file_path\": \"src/Parser_Decl_StructEnum.cpp\", \"start_line\": 757, \"end_line\": 788, \"side\": \"RIGHT\"} -->\n\n🚩 **Nested enum registration only handles enums directly inside struct body, not via typedef/using**\n\nThe new qualified-name registration logic at `src/Parser_Decl_StructEnum.cpp:757-788` only fires when the `enum` keyword is encountered directly inside the struct member parsing loop (line 746). Enums declared through `typedef` or `using` aliases (handled separately at lines 796 and 805) do not get the same qualified-name registration treatment. The old Known Issues section in MISSING_FEATURES.md mentioned `parse_member_type_alias` as a potential site needing the same fix. This may not matter in practice if those paths don't create new enum types, but it's worth verifying that ADL for typedef'd nested enums also works.\n\n<!-- devin-review-badge-begin -->\n<a href=\"https://app.devin.ai/review/gregorgullwi/flashcpp/pull/943\" target=\"_blank\">\n  <picture>\n    <source media=\"(prefers-color-scheme: dark)\" srcset=\"https://static.devin.ai/assets/gh-open-in-devin-review-dark.svg?v=1\">\n    <img src=\"https://static.devin.ai/assets/gh-open-in-devin-review-light.svg?v=1\" alt=\"Open in Devin Review\">\n  </picture>\n</a>\n<!-- devin-review-badge-end -->\n\n---\n*Was this helpful? React with 👍 or 👎 to provide feedback.*","path":"src/Parser_Decl_StructEnum.cpp","line":788,"author":"devin-ai-integration","created_at":"2026-03-18T15:59:51Z","updated_at":"2026-03-18T15:59:53Z","html_url":"https://github.com/GregorGullwi/FlashCpp/pull/943#discussion_r2954520529"}],"total_count":1},{"is_resolved":false,"is_outdated":false,"is_collapsed":false,"comments":[{"body":"<!-- devin-review-comment {\"id\": \"ANALYSIS_pr-review-job-8da89a4ee66e4231bc21ac43eba03936_0003\", \"file_path\": \"src/Parser_Decl_StructEnum.cpp\", \"start_line\": 770, \"end_line\": 776, \"side\": \"RIGHT\"} -->\n\n🚩 **struct_parsing_context_stack_ stores simple names — correct for building struct-chain but differs from nested class qualified name logic**\n\nThe struct context is pushed at `src/Parser_Decl_StructEnum.cpp:177-183` with the simple `struct_name` (not the qualified name built at lines 105-108 for nested classes). The new enum registration code at line 771 iterates the full stack to reconstruct `A::B::C::E`. This works correctly because each stack entry holds only the simple name and the loop concatenates them with `::`. However, this approach diverges from how nested class names are built (line 103-108), which only looks at the *back* of the stack to form `Parent::Child`. For 3+ levels of nesting, the nested class qualified names would be `B::C` (from `struct_parsing_context_stack_.back()`), not `A::B::C`. This is fine for the enum case since it walks the full stack, but suggests the nested class name-building at line 103-108 may not generalize to deeply nested structs (pre-existing, not introduced by this PR).\n\n<!-- devin-review-badge-begin -->\n<a href=\"https://app.devin.ai/review/gregorgullwi/flashcpp/pull/943\" target=\"_blank\">\n  <picture>\n    <source media=\"(prefers-color-scheme: dark)\" srcset=\"https://static.devin.ai/assets/gh-open-in-devin-review-dark.svg?v=1\">\n    <img src=\"https://static.devin.ai/assets/gh-open-in-devin-review-light.svg?v=1\" alt=\"Open in Devin Review\">\n  </picture>\n</a>\n<!-- devin-review-badge-end -->\n\n---\n*Was this helpful? React with 👍 or 👎 to provide feedback.*","path":"src/Parser_Decl_StructEnum.cpp","line":776,"author":"devin-ai-integration","created_at":"2026-03-18T16:00:28Z","updated_at":"2026-03-18T16:00:29Z","html_url":"https://github.com/GregorGullwi/FlashCpp/pull/943#discussion_r2954526199"}],"total_count":1},{"is_resolved":false,"is_outdated":false,"is_collapsed":false,"comments":[{"body":"<!-- devin-review-comment {\"id\": \"ANALYSIS_pr-review-job-8da89a4ee66e4231bc21ac43eba03936_0001\", \"file_path\": \"src/OverloadResolution.h\", \"start_line\": 1487, \"end_line\": 1491, \"side\": \"RIGHT\"} -->\n\n🚩 **Dedup may include duplicate candidates when functions lack mangled names**\n\nAt `src/OverloadResolution.h:1489`, the dedup logic deliberately includes ADL candidates that have no mangled name (`!fd.has_mangled_name()`), since it cannot reliably determine identity. The comment at line 1487-1488 acknowledges this. If both `lookup_all` and `lookup_adl` return the same function declaration node and neither has a mangled name, it would appear twice in `overloads`, potentially causing a false ambiguity result at line 1555. In practice, most operator overloads will have mangled names by the time overload resolution runs, so this is unlikely to trigger. But it's worth noting as a latent edge case.\n\n<!-- devin-review-badge-begin -->\n<a href=\"https://app.devin.ai/review/gregorgullwi/flashcpp/pull/943\" target=\"_blank\">\n  <picture>\n    <source media=\"(prefers-color-scheme: dark)\" srcset=\"https://static.devin.ai/assets/gh-open-in-devin-review-dark.svg?v=1\">\n    <img src=\"https://static.devin.ai/assets/gh-open-in-devin-review-light.svg?v=1\" alt=\"Open in Devin Review\">\n  </picture>\n</a>\n<!-- devin-review-badge-end -->\n\n---\n*Was this helpful? 2026-03-18T16:06:03.1472728Z 
2026-03-18T16:06:03.1473853Z copilot: Now I have a clear picture of the 3 unresolved Devin comments. Let me analyze each one and check the current code state:
2026-03-18T16:06:03.1479320Z 
2026-03-18T16:06:03.1479619Z copilot: Let me analyze the unresolved Devin comments:
2026-03-18T16:06:03.1480028Z 
2026-03-18T16:06:03.1481082Z 1. **Unresolved - `Parser_Decl_StructEnum.cpp:757-788`**: Nested enum registration only handles enums directly inside struct body, not via typedef/using. Says to check `parse_member_type_alias`.
2026-03-18T16:06:03.1482402Z 
2026-03-18T16:06:03.1484605Z 2. **Unresolved - `Parser_Decl_StructEnum.cpp:770-776`**: `struct_parsing_context_stack_` stores simple names, diverges from nested class qualified name logic. The nested class name-building at line 103-108 only looks at the back of the stack (for `Parent::Child`), not the full stack for deeply nested structs. This is a pre-existing issue.
2026-03-18T16:06:03.1487002Z    
2026-03-18T16:06:03.1487615Z 3. **Unresolved - `OverloadResolution.h:1487-1491`**: Dedup may include duplicate candidates when functions lack mangled names. This is a latent edge case.
2026-03-18T16:06:03.1488365Z 
2026-03-18T16:06:03.1490184Z The GregorGullwi comment says I need to look at Devin's unresolved comments and implement any missing features, regardless if they're pre-existing. So I need to fix the struct context stack to build qualified names correctly for deeply nested structs, handle typedef/using alias enums to get proper qualified names, and address the dedup issue with missing mangled names.
2026-03-18T16:06:03.1492453Z 
2026-03-18T16:06:03.1497815Z Let me start by examining the nested class name-building problem more closely. The current code only uses the back of the struct parsing context stack, but for deeply nested structures I need to traverse the full chain to construct the complete qualified name. I'm realizing that typedef or using aliases for enum types don't actually create new types in C++ — they're just aliases pointing to the existing enum. So I wouldn't need to register a separate TypeInfo for them. The tricky part is whether ADL lookup would resolve the alias name or the underlying enum name when matching function parameters. Let me think through how this works in practice with a concrete example.
2026-03-18T16:06:03.1500443Z 
2026-03-18T16:06:03.1502195Z For ADL purposes, the associated namespace is determined by the actual enum type, not the alias. So if I have a function parameter typed as `Container::MyStatus`, the ADL lookup still uses the namespace associated with `Container::Status`. The real question is whether the parser correctly resolves `Container::MyStatus` to the underlying `Container::Status` type when registering function signatures, and whether `gTypesByName` has entries for both the alias and the original type name.
2026-03-18T16:06:03.1504040Z 
2026-03-18T16:06:03.1504867Z I need to check what `parse_member_type_alias` actually does to understand how type aliases are being registered. Then I should look at the deduplication logic for functions without mangled names to see if there's an issue there.
2026-03-18T16:06:03.1506032Z 
2026-03-18T16:06:03.1508099Z The dedup check currently allows functions through if they lack a mangled name, which could let duplicates slip in. A fallback approach would be to use the function's source name combined with its parameter signature for deduplication when a mangled name isn't available. But since Devin mentioned this is unlikely to happen in practice, the more pressing issues are getting qualified names for typedef/using aliases into `gTypesByName`, fixing the nested class name-building to handle deeper nesting properly, and addressing the dedup gap for unmangled functions.
2026-03-18T16:06:03.1510207Z 
2026-03-18T16:06:03.1516444Z For the nested class problem specifically, when we're parsing deeply nested structures like `ns::A::B::C`, the qualified name gets built incorrectly—it becomes `ns::B::C` instead of `ns::A::B::C` because the name-building logic only looks at the current context stack's back element. Let me trace through the context stack to understand how this actually plays out and whether it's genuinely broken. The context stack stores only simple names, so when building qualified names for nested types like enums, walking the stack produces the correct fully-qualified path—but the struct's own `type_name` still gets constructed incorrectly from just the back of the stack, missing the full nesting hierarchy.
2026-03-18T16:06:03.1520905Z 
2026-03-18T16:06:03.1531166Z I need to apply the same stack-walking approach to nested class registration itself, building the complete qualified name by iterating through all contexts rather than relying on just the immediate parent. I see the issue now—when a nested class is inside a namespaced struct, I need to prepend the full qualified name of the parent, not just its simple struct name. So for `B` nested in `ns::A`, the type_name should be "ns::A::B", which means I need to use the parent's full qualified type_name rather than just its struct_name field. I see the core issue now—when we nest struct B inside struct A which is in namespace ns, the context stack loses the namespace qualifier, so B gets registered as "A::B" instead of "ns::A::B". The real problem surfaces when we try to resolve a fully qualified call like `f(ns::A::B::E::X)` because the type lookup doesn't match what was actually stored during parsing. out is that the nested class name-building logic at line 103-108 only uses the back of the stack, so it would produce incorrect qualified names for deeply nested structs (like B::C instead of A::B::C), whereas the enum registration code correctly walks the full stack to reconstruct the complete path. This is a pre-existing limitation that doesn't affect the enum fix but suggests the nested class handling might need broader attention.
2026-03-18T16:06:03.1539024Z 
2026-03-18T16:06:03.1541463Z Now I'm looking at the typedef and using aliases inside structs—specifically whether `typedef enum E MyAlias` or `using MyAlias = E` should register `Container::MyAlias` in gTypesByName as an alias pointing to the same TypeInfo as the underlying type.
2026-03-18T16:06:03.1543186Z 
2026-03-18T16:06:03.1545430Z For the nested struct qualified name issue, when parsing deeply nested structs like `A::B::C`, the context stack only preserves the immediate parent, so `C` gets registered as `B::C` instead of `A::B::C`—this is the real bug I need to fix.
2026-03-18T16:06:03.1547075Z 
2026-03-18T16:06:03.1571102Z The dedup issue in OverloadResolution.h is a minor edge case where functions without mangled names might slip through, but I should prioritize fixing the nested struct naming first since that's the most impactful. So when `is_nested_class = true`, we're setting `full_qualified_name` to just "B::C" instead of the complete path "ns::A::B::C" — we skip the namespace qualification entirely. And looking at the registration code, we're only storing the struct under its simple name "C" in the type registry, not under any of its qualified forms, which means lookups for the fully qualified name won't work for deeply nested classes.
2026-03-18T16:06:03.1574856Z 
2026-03-18T16:06:03.1576580Z I should write a test to confirm this is actually breaking in practice, then fix the qualified name building to properly walk the entire context stack, verify the typedef and using handling, and address the mangled name situation.

2026-03-18T16:06:03.1635589Z     755. 					if (enum_it != gTypesByName.end()) {
2026-03-18T16:06:03.1635992Z     756. 						struct_info->addNestedEnumIndex(enum_it->second->type_index_);
2026-03-18T16:06:03.1636350Z     757. 
2026-03-18T16:06:03.1636642Z     758. 						// Register the enum under every qualified name a caller might use.
2026-03-18T16:06:03.1637107Z     759. 						// Per C++20 [basic.lookup.qual], a nested enum is accessed by
2026-03-18T16:06:03.1637520Z     760. 						// qualifying through the enclosing class name(s).
2026-03-18T16:06:03.1637935Z     761. 						// Per [basic.lookup.argdep]/2 the associated namespace is the
2026-03-18T16:06:03.1638335Z     762. 						// innermost enclosing namespace.
2026-03-18T16:06:03.1638608Z     763. 						//
2026-03-18T16:06:03.1638902Z     764. 						// Walk struct_parsing_context_stack_ (which already includes the
2026-03-18T16:06:03.1639364Z     765. 						// current struct pushed above) from outermost to innermost to
2026-03-18T16:06:03.1639822Z     766. 						// build the full struct chain.  This correctly handles any depth
2026-03-18T16:06:03.1640203Z     767. 						// of struct nesting, e.g.:
2026-03-18T16:06:03.1640522Z     768. 						//   ns::A::B::C::E   (3 levels: A > B > C, enum E)
2026-03-18T16:06:03.1640861Z     769. 						//   ns::Container::Status (1 level)
2026-03-18T16:06:03.1641180Z     770. 						StringBuilder struct_chain_builder;
2026-03-18T16:06:03.1641545Z     771. 						for (const auto& ctx : struct_parsing_context_stack_) {
2026-03-18T16:06:03.1641975Z     772. 							struct_chain_builder.append(ctx.struct_name).append("::");
2026-03-18T16:06:03.1642322Z     773. 						}
2026-03-18T16:06:03.1642577Z     774. 						struct_chain_builder.append(enum_decl.name());
2026-03-18T16:06:03.1643053Z     775. 						StringHandle struct_relative_handle = StringTable::getOrInternStringHandle(
2026-03-18T16:06:03.1643512Z     776. 							struct_chain_builder.commit());
2026-03-18T16:06:03.1643927Z     777. 						// Register struct-relative name ("A::B::C::E", "Container::Status")
2026-03-18T16:06:03.1644426Z     778. 						gTypesByName.emplace(struct_relative_handle, enum_it->second);
2026-03-18T16:06:03.1644797Z     779. 
2026-03-18T16:06:03.1645349Z     780. 						// Also register namespace-fully-qualified name using NamespaceHandle
2026-03-18T16:06:03.1645798Z     781. 						// ("ns::A::B::C::E", "ns::Container::Status")
2026-03-18T16:06:03.1646131Z     782. 						if (!qualified_namespace.empty()) {
2026-03-18T16:06:03.1646607Z     783. 							StringHandle ns_qualified_handle = gNamespaceRegistry.buildQualifiedIdentifier(
2026-03-18T16:06:03.1647129Z     784. 								current_namespace_handle, struct_relative_handle);
2026-03-18T16:06:03.1647524Z     785. 							if (ns_qualified_handle != struct_relative_handle) {
2026-03-18T16:06:03.1647951Z     786. 								gTypesByName.emplace(ns_qualified_handle, enum_it->second);
2026-03-18T16:06:03.1648308Z     787. 							}
2026-03-18T16:06:03.1648648Z     788. 						}
2026-03-18T16:06:03.1648839Z     789. 					}
2026-03-18T16:06:03.1649016Z     790. 				}
2026-03-18T16:06:03.1649298Z     791. 				// The semicolon is already consumed by parse_enum_declaration
2026-03-18T16:06:03.1649648Z     792. 				continue;
2026-03-18T16:06:03.1649851Z     793. 			}
2026-03-18T16:06:03.1650025Z     794. 
2026-03-18T16:06:03.1650243Z     795. 			// Check for 'using' keyword - type alias
2026-03-18T16:06:03.1650542Z     796. 			if (keyword == "using") {
2026-03-18T16:06:03.1650954Z     797. 				auto alias_result = parse_member_type_alias("using", &struct_ref, current_access);
2026-03-18T16:06:03.1651402Z     798. 				if (alias_result.is_error()) {
2026-03-18T16:06:03.1651676Z     799. 					return alias_result;
2026-03-18T16:06:03.1651911Z     800. 				}
2026-03-18T16:06:03.1652091Z     801. 				continue;
2026-03-18T16:06:03.1652285Z     802. 			}
2026-03-18T16:06:03.1652458Z     803. 
2026-03-18T16:06:03.1652833Z     804. 			// Check for 'typedef' keyword - type alias (C-style)
2026-03-18T16:06:03.1653174Z     805. 			if (keyword == "typedef") {
2026-03-18T16:06:22.1255797Z 
2026-03-18T16:06:22.1256602Z copilot: Let me look at the unresolved issues and the current code more thoroughly:
2026-03-18T16:06:22.1257230Z 
2026-03-18T16:06:22.1257500Z copilot: Now I see the three issues clearly. Let me plan:
2026-03-18T16:06:22.1257929Z 
2026-03-18T16:06:22.1258559Z 1. **Issue 2 (nested class qualified name - 3+ levels)**: Fix lines 100-116 to walk the full context stack like we do for enums.
2026-03-18T16:06:22.1259992Z 2. **Issue 1 (typedef/using enum aliases)**: Check what `parse_member_type_alias` returns and whether it needs the same treatment.
2026-03-18T16:06:22.1261325Z 3. **Issue 3 (no-mangled-name dedup)**: Consider if we can use function name + source location as fallback.
2026-03-18T16:06:22.1261752Z

2026-03-18T16:06:22.1510449Z     1470. 
2026-03-18T16:06:22.1510919Z     1471. 		// Build a set of existing mangled names for O(1) dedup.
2026-03-18T16:06:22.1511698Z     1472. 		// Using std::string_view is safe: mangled names are interned in
2026-03-18T16:06:22.1512479Z     1473. 		// stable ChunkedStringAllocator storage (they never move).
2026-03-18T16:06:22.1513128Z     1474. 		std::unordered_set<std::string_view> existing_mangled;
2026-03-18T16:06:22.1513609Z     1475. 		existing_mangled.reserve(overloads.size());
2026-03-18T16:06:22.1514238Z     1476. 		for (const auto& node : overloads) {
2026-03-18T16:06:22.1514834Z     1477. 			if (node.is<FunctionDeclarationNode>()) {
2026-03-18T16:06:22.1515750Z     1478. 				const auto& fd = node.as<FunctionDeclarationNode>();
2026-03-18T16:06:22.1516386Z     1479. 				if (fd.has_mangled_name()) {
2026-03-18T16:06:22.1516961Z     1480. 					existing_mangled.insert(fd.mangled_name());
2026-03-18T16:06:22.1517493Z     1481. 				}
2026-03-18T16:06:22.1517813Z     1482. 			}
2026-03-18T16:06:22.1518111Z     1483. 		}
2026-03-18T16:06:22.1518317Z     1484. 		for (auto& cand : adl_candidates) {
2026-03-18T16:06:22.1518654Z     1485. 			if (cand.is<FunctionDeclarationNode>()) {
2026-03-18T16:06:22.1519019Z     1486. 				const auto& fd = cand.as<FunctionDeclarationNode>();
2026-03-18T16:06:22.1519448Z     1487. 				// If the function has no mangled name we cannot deduplicate it
2026-03-18T16:06:22.1519916Z     1488. 				// reliably; include it so we don't silently drop valid candidates.
2026-03-18T16:06:22.1520719Z     1489. 				if (!fd.has_mangled_name() || existing_mangled.insert(fd.mangled_name()).second) {
2026-03-18T16:06:22.1521180Z     1490. 					overloads.push_back(std::move(cand));
2026-03-18T16:06:22.1521459Z     1491. 				}
2026-03-18T16:06:22.1521640Z     1492. 			} else {
2026-03-18T16:06:22.1521876Z     1493. 				overloads.push_back(std::move(cand));
2026-03-18T16:06:22.1522148Z     1494. 			}
2026-03-18T16:06:22.1522317Z     1495. 		}
2026-03-18T16:06:22.1522490Z     1496. 	}
2026-03-18T16:06:22.1522655Z     1497.

2026-03-18T16:06:22.1527070Z     1508.

2026-03-18T16:06:22.1527725Z     1510. 		const auto& p1_type = params[1].as<DeclarationNode>().type_node();

2026-03-18T16:06:27.0996984Z     7. 
2026-03-18T16:06:27.1000198Z     8. ParseResult Parser::parse_member_type_alias(std::string_view keyword, StructDeclarationNode* struct_ref, AccessSpecifier current_access)
2026-03-18T16:06:27.1001315Z     9. {
2026-03-18T16:06:27.1001693Z     10. 	advance(); // consume 'typedef' or 'using'
2026-03-18T16:06:27.1002182Z     11. 	
2026-03-18T16:06:27.1002644Z     12. 	// For 'using', check if it's an alias or a using-declaration
2026-03-18T16:06:27.1003530Z     13. 	if (keyword == "using") {
2026-03-18T16:06:27.1004045Z     14. 		auto alias_token = peek_info();
2026-03-18T16:06:27.1004565Z     15. 		if (!alias_token.kind().is_identifier()) {
2026-03-18T16:06:27.1005575Z     16. 			return ParseResult::error("Expected alias name after 'using'", peek_info());
2026-03-18T16:06:27.1006279Z     17. 		}
2026-03-18T16:06:27.1006570Z     18. 		
2026-03-18T16:06:27.1006893Z     19. 		// Look ahead to see if this is:
2026-03-18T16:06:27.1007517Z     20. 		// 1. Type alias: using Alias = Type;  (identifier followed by '=')
2026-03-18T16:06:27.1008388Z     21. 		// 2. Using-declaration: using namespace::member;  (identifier followed by '::')
2026-03-18T16:06:27.1009446Z     22. 		// 3. Inheriting constructor: using Base<T>::Base;  (identifier<template args> followed by '::')
2026-03-18T16:06:27.1010350Z     23. 		SaveHandle lookahead_pos = save_token_position();
2026-03-18T16:06:27.1010938Z     24. 		advance(); // consume first identifier
2026-03-18T16:06:27.1011411Z     25. 		
2026-03-18T16:06:27.1011791Z     26. 		// Skip template arguments if present
2026-03-18T16:06:27.1012275Z     27. 		if (peek() == "<"_tok) {
2026-03-18T16:06:27.1012714Z     28. 			skip_template_arguments();
2026-03-18T16:06:27.1013132Z     29. 		}
2026-03-18T16:06:27.1013415Z     30. 		
2026-03-18T16:06:27.1013729Z     31. 		auto next_token = peek_info();
2026-03-18T16:06:27.1014156Z     32. 		
2026-03-18T16:06:27.1014494Z     33. 		if (next_token.kind() == "::"_tok) {
2026-03-18T16:06:27.1015469Z     34. 			// This is a using-declaration like: using std::__is_integer<_Tp>::__value;
2026-03-18T16:06:27.1016305Z     35. 			// Or an inheriting constructor like: using Base<T>::Base;
2026-03-18T16:06:27.1017096Z     36. 			// Parse and extract the member name to register it in the current scope
2026-03-18T16:06:27.1018165Z     37. 			std::string_view base_class_name = alias_token.value();  // Remember the first identifier (base class name)
2026-03-18T16:06:27.1019058Z     38. 			std::string_view member_name;
2026-03-18T16:06:27.1019511Z     39. 			
2026-03-18T16:06:27.1019835Z     40. 			while (peek() == "::"_tok) {
2026-03-18T16:06:27.1020296Z     41. 				advance(); // consume '::'
2026-03-18T16:06:27.1020727Z     42. 				
2026-03-18T16:06:27.1021155Z     43. 				// Consume the next identifier, operator, or template
2026-03-18T16:06:27.1021725Z     44. 				if (!peek().is_eof()) {
2026-03-18T16:06:27.1022171Z     45. 					if (peek().is_identifier()) {
2026-03-18T16:06:27.1022917Z     46. 						member_name = peek_info().value();  // Track last identifier as potential member name
2026-03-18T16:06:27.1023716Z     47. 						advance(); // consume identifier
2026-03-18T16:06:27.1024161Z     48. 						
2026-03-18T16:06:27.1024501Z     49. 						// Check for template arguments
2026-03-18T16:06:27.1024972Z     50. 						if (peek() == "<"_tok) {
2026-03-18T16:06:27.1025629Z     51. 							skip_template_arguments();
2026-03-18T16:06:27.1026255Z     52. 							// After template args, the member name is whatever comes next
2026-03-18T16:06:27.1027361Z     53. 							member_name = "";  // Reset - next identifier after :: will be the member
2026-03-18T16:06:27.1028010Z     54. 						}
2026-03-18T16:06:27.1028381Z     55. 					} else if (peek() == "operator"_tok) {
2026-03-18T16:06:27.1028984Z     56. 						// using Base::operator Type; (conversion operator)
2026-03-18T16:06:27.1029612Z     57. 						// using Base::operator=; (assignment operator)
2026-03-18T16:06:27.1030169Z     58. 						advance(); // consume 'operator'
2026-03-18T16:06:27.1030995Z     59. 						// Build the full operator name: "operator=", "operator<<", "operator __integral_type", etc.
2026-03-18T16:06:27.1031814Z     60. 						StringBuilder op_name_builder;
2026-03-18T16:06:27.1032308Z     61. 						op_name_builder.append("operator");
2026-03-18T16:06:27.1032936Z     62. 						while (!peek().is_eof() && peek() != ";"_tok && peek() != "..."_tok) {
2026-03-18T16:06:27.1033720Z     63. 							// Add space before type names but not before operator symbols
2026-03-18T16:06:27.1034651Z     64. 							if (peek().is_identifier() || peek().is_keyword()) {
2026-03-18T16:06:27.1035467Z     65. 								op_name_builder.append(" ");
2026-03-18T16:06:27.1035926Z     66. 							}
2026-03-18T16:06:27.1036349Z     67. 							op_name_builder.append(peek_info().value());
2026-03-18T16:06:27.1036880Z     68. 							advance();
2026-03-18T16:06:27.1037246Z     69. 						}
2026-03-18T16:06:27.1037637Z     70. 						member_name = op_name_builder.commit();
2026-03-18T16:06:27.1038134Z     71. 						break;
2026-03-18T16:06:27.1038471Z     72. 					} else {
2026-03-18T16:06:27.1038811Z     73. 						break;
2026-03-18T16:06:27.1039141Z     74. 					}
2026-03-18T16:06:27.1039440Z     75. 				}
2026-03-18T16:06:27.1039739Z     76. 			}
2026-03-18T16:06:27.1040042Z     77. 			
2026-03-18T16:06:27.1040528Z     78. 			// Check if this is an inheriting constructor: using Base::Base;
2026-03-18T16:06:27.1041434Z     79. 			// Per C++ standard, inheriting constructors specifically require the member name
2026-03-18T16:06:27.1042447Z     80. 			// to match the base class name. General using-declarations can import any member.
2026-03-18T16:06:36.2543051Z

2026-03-18T16:07:12.2363474Z 
2026-03-18T16:07:12.2364680Z copilot: So `register_type_alias` creates a new TypeInfo and registers it in gTypesByName with the alias name. That means if you have:
2026-03-18T16:07:12.2366030Z ```cpp
2026-03-18T16:07:12.2366401Z namespace ns {
2026-03-18T16:07:12.2366777Z     struct Container {
2026-03-18T16:07:12.2367214Z         enum Status { Ok, Fail };
2026-03-18T16:07:12.2370108Z         using MyStatus = Status;
2026-03-18T16:07:12.2370565Z     };
2026-03-18T16:07:12.2370868Z }
2026-03-18T16:07:12.2371149Z ```
2026-03-18T16:07:12.2371345Z 
2026-03-18T16:07:12.2371838Z When `using MyStatus = Status` is parsed inside the struct, `register_type_alias` would be called with something like:
2026-03-18T16:07:12.2372763Z - name = "MyStatus" (simple, not qualified)
2026-03-18T16:07:12.2373297Z - type_spec = the type spec for Status
2026-03-18T16:07:12.2373547Z 
2026-03-18T16:07:12.2374348Z So "MyStatus" is registered in gTypesByName, but "Container::MyStatus" is NOT registered. 
2026-03-18T16:07:12.2374962Z 
2026-03-18T16:07:12.2376887Z This is exactly the bug Devin identified. But I'm noticing there's a `qualified_alias_name` function around line 397-403 in Parser_Decl_TypedefUsing.cpp that looks like it might be building qualified names—let me check what that's actually doing.
2026-03-18T16:07:12.2392645Z 
2026-03-18T16:07:12.2392968Z function:
2026-03-18T16:07:12.2393369Z   name: view

2026-03-18T16:07:26.8708246Z copilot: So for `using` type aliases inside struct bodies, the `qualified_alias_name` is built as `struct_ref->name() + "::" + alias_name`. But `struct_ref->name()` returns the *simple* struct name, not the fully-qualified name!
2026-03-18T16:07:26.8709415Z

2026-03-18T16:07:36.6753194Z     765. 		double doubleValue() const { return std::holds_alternative<double>(value) ? std::get<double>(value) : 0.0; }

2026-03-18T16:08:40.9058679Z 
2026-03-18T16:08:40.9059362Z copilot: Let me check the TypeInfo structure and how type alias lookup works:
2026-03-18T16:08:40.9062651Z 
2026-03-18T16:08:40.9064299Z copilot: So `StructDeclarationNode::name()` returns the `name_` StringHandle which is the SIMPLE name (the struct was created with `emplace_node_ref<StructDeclarationNode>(struct_name, is_class)` where `struct_name` is the simple, unqualified name).
2026-03-18T16:08:40.9066151Z 
2026-03-18T16:08:40.9066515Z So in `parse_member_type_alias`, the `qualified_alias_name` at line 397-403:
2026-03-18T16:08:40.9067177Z ```cpp
2026-03-18T16:08:40.9067485Z if (struct_ref) {
2026-03-18T16:08:40.9067908Z     StringBuilder qualified_builder;
2026-03-18T16:08:40.9068439Z     qualified_builder.append(struct_ref->name());
2026-03-18T16:08:40.9068995Z     qualified_builder.append("::");
2026-03-18T16:08:40.9069311Z 
2026-03-18T16:08:40.9069317Z 
2026-03-18T16:08:40.9069443Z ```
2026-03-18T16:08:40.9069600Z 
2026-03-18T16:08:40.9070722Z This builds `struct_simple_name::alias_name` - so for `using MyStatus = Status` inside `Container`, it builds "Container::MyStatus". But if `Container` is in namespace `ns`, the full qualified name would need to be "ns::Container::MyStatus".
2026-03-18T16:08:40.9071971Z 
2026-03-18T16:08:40.9073085Z However, looking at how `register_type_alias` works, it registers with just the alias name, which means the qualified name construction here might not be capturing the full namespace scope.
2026-03-18T16:08:40.9074369Z 
2026-03-18T16:08:40.9076875Z Now I'm thinking about this from the ADL perspective - when you have a function like `void f(Container::MyStatus s)`, the type lookup would search for "Container::MyStatus". But the real issue Devin is pointing out is that enums declared directly get registered with their qualified names like "Container::Status", while type aliases created with `using` or `typedef` don't receive the same treatment - they should also be registered as "Container::AliasStatus" to maintain consistency.
2026-03-18T16:08:40.9079378Z 
2026-03-18T16:08:40.9079464Z ```
2026-03-18T16:08:40.9079670Z 
2026-03-18T16:08:40.9079744Z cpp
2026-03-18T16:08:40.9079923Z struct Container {
2026-03-18T16:08:40.9080237Z     enum Status { Ok, Fail };  // gets registered as "Container::Status"
2026-03-18T16:08:40.9080898Z     using AliasStatus = Status;  // should also get registered as "Container::AliasStatus"
2026-03-18T16:08:40.9081441Z };
2026-03-18T16:08:40.9081604Z ```
2026-03-18T16:08:40.9081697Z 
2026-03-18T16:08:40.9083408Z Looking at the `parse_member_type_alias` function, it does construct and register the qualified alias name like "Container::AliasStatus" when there's a struct context. But the problem is it only uses the simple struct name, not the fully qualified namespace path - so if Container is nested in namespace `ns`, it registers "Container::AliasStatus" instead of "ns::Container::AliasStatus".
2026-03-18T16:08:40.9085248Z 
2026-03-18T16:08:40.9086380Z The enum fix already handles this by building the full context stack and using the namespace registry to prepend the namespace. For typedef and using aliases, though, the real question is whether it matters for ADL - what actually matters is the underlying type of the argument, not the alias itself.
2026-03-18T16:08:40.9087640Z 
2026-03-18T16:08:40.9089595Z When you pass a `Container::AliasStatus` argument, ADL looks at the actual type it refers to (`Container::Status`), which lives in namespace `ns`. The problem emerges during overload resolution: if a function parameter expects `Container::AliasStatus` but we're matching against `Container::Status`, they need to resolve to the same TypeInfo. Since `register_type_alias` creates a new TypeInfo entry with its own type_index_, these would be treated as different types, which breaks the matching.
2026-03-18T16:08:40.9091581Z 
2026-03-18T16:08:40.9093030Z Looking at the implementation, the alias TypeInfo gets created with the original type's `type_index_` field, but it occupies a new position in the gTypeInfo vector—so while the type_index_ field points to the original type, the TypeInfo object itself is distinct.
2026-03-18T16:08:41.4147141Z 
2026-03-18T16:08:41.4147616Z function:
2026-03-18T16:08:41.4148005Z   name: bash
2026-03-18T16:08:41.4148365Z   args:
2026-03-18T16:08:41.4150039Z     command: cd /home/runner/work/FlashCpp/FlashCpp && grep -n "struct TypeInfo\|class TypeInfo" src/*.h | head -3
2026-03-18T16:08:41.4150726Z     description: Find TypeInfo struct
2026-03-18T16:08:41.4151039Z   result: |
2026-03-18T16:08:41.4151317Z     src/AstNodeTypes_DeclNodes.h:711:struct TypeInfo
2026-03-18T16:08:41.4151736Z     src/ConstExprEvaluator.h:16:struct TypeInfo;
2026-03-18T16:08:41.4152176Z     src/ParserScopeGuards.h:15:struct TypeInfo;
2026-03-18T16:08:41.4152537Z     <exited with exit code 0>

2026-03-18T16:08:47.6804189Z     725. 	// For struct types, store additional information

2026-03-18T16:08:47.6805900Z     729. 	std::unique_ptr<EnumTypeInfo> enum_info_;
2026-03-18T16:08:47.6806193Z     730. 
2026-03-18T16:08:47.6806472Z     731. 	// For typedef, store the size in bits (for primitive types)
2026-03-18T16:08:47.6806932Z     732. 	int type_size_ = 0;  // Changed from unsigned char to int for large types
2026-03-18T16:08:47.6807294Z     733. 
2026-03-18T16:08:47.6807544Z     734. 	// For typedef of pointer types, store the pointer depth

2026-03-18T16:08:47.6808137Z     736.

2026-03-18T16:08:47.6809269Z     739.

2026-03-18T16:08:47.6810383Z     742. 	
2026-03-18T16:08:47.6810675Z     743. 	// For template instantiations: store metadata to avoid name parsing
2026-03-18T16:08:47.6811145Z     744. 	// If base_template_ is valid, this type is a template instantiation
2026-03-18T16:08:47.6811680Z     745. 	QualifiedIdentifier base_template_;  // e.g., {std, "vector"} for std::vector<int>
2026-03-18T16:08:47.6812096Z     746. 	
2026-03-18T16:08:47.6812484Z     747. 	// Lightweight storage for template argument type indices (avoids TemplateTypeArg dependency)
2026-03-18T16:08:47.6813048Z     748. 	// For type arguments: stores TypeIndex (index into gTypeInfo)
2026-03-18T16:08:47.6813587Z     749. 	// For non-type arguments: stores the value directly (supports int64_t, double, StringHandle)
2026-03-18T16:08:47.6814054Z     750. 	struct TemplateArgInfo {
2026-03-18T16:08:47.6814375Z     751. 		Type base_type = Type::Invalid;  // For primitive types
2026-03-18T16:08:47.6814787Z     752. 		TypeIndex type_index {};        // For user-defined types
2026-03-18T16:08:47.6815355Z     753. 		InlineVector<CVQualifier, 4> pointer_cv_qualifiers;
2026-03-18T16:08:47.6815761Z     754. 		size_t pointer_depth = 0;        // Pointer indirection level
2026-03-18T16:08:47.6816255Z     755. 		CVQualifier cv_qualifier = CVQualifier::None;  // cv-qualifiers on the argument
2026-03-18T16:08:47.6816775Z     756. 		ReferenceQualifier ref_qualifier = ReferenceQualifier::None;
2026-03-18T16:08:47.6817308Z     757. 		std::variant<int64_t, double, StringHandle> value = int64_t{0};  // For non-type arguments
2026-03-18T16:08:47.6817849Z     758. 		bool is_value = false;           // true if this is a non-type argument
2026-03-18T16:08:47.6818215Z     759. 		bool is_array = false;
2026-03-18T16:08:47.6818509Z     760. 		std::optional<size_t> array_size = std::nullopt;
2026-03-18T16:09:24.7434235Z 
2026-03-18T16:09:24.7434930Z copilot: Let me verify these issues with concrete tests before fixing them:
2026-03-18T16:09:24.7437911Z 
2026-03-18T16:09:24.7439446Z copilot: So `TypeInfo::type_index_` is the index of the original type. For a type alias, it stores the original type's index. This allows resolving the alias back to the original type.
2026-03-18T16:09:24.7440547Z 
2026-03-18T16:09:24.7441228Z So for `using AliasStatus = Status`:- A new TypeInfo is created with `type_ = Type::Enum` and `type_index_ = <original Status TypeIndex>`
2026-03-18T16:09:24.7442544Z - This is registered in gTypesByName as "Container::AliasStatus" (simple struct name)
2026-03-18T16:09:24.7443154Z 
2026-03-18T16:09:24.7444272Z Now, the question is: does the ADL parameter type lookup work? When checking if a function `void f(Container::AliasStatus s)` can be called with an argument of type `Container::Status`, the lookup does:
2026-03-18T16:09:24.7445505Z 
2026-03-18T16:09:24.7445510Z 
2026-03-18T16:09:24.7446305Z 1. Look up "Container::AliasStatus" in gTypesByName → gets TypeInfo with type_ = Enum, type_index_ = <Status TypeIndex>
2026-03-18T16:09:24.7447553Z 2. Look up "Container::Status" in gTypesByName → gets TypeInfo with type_ = Enum, type_index_ = <Status TypeIndex>
2026-03-18T16:09:24.7448313Z 3. These have the same type_index_, so they should match.
2026-03-18T16:09:24.7448557Z 
2026-03-18T16:09:24.7449699Z However, there's a problem: if `Container` is nested in namespace `ns`, the lookup for "Container::AliasStatus" fails because it actually needs to search for "ns::Container::AliasStatus" instead. This is the same namespace resolution issue we're already addressing for enums.
2026-03-18T16:09:24.7450822Z 
2026-03-18T16:09:24.7452088Z The real problem is that `parse_member_type_alias` uses `struct_ref->name()` which only gives the simple name, not the fully qualified name with namespace. When we register a typedef like `using MyStatus = Status` inside `ns::Container`, we need to store it under the complete qualified name so lookups work correctly.
2026-03-18T16:09:24.7453349Z 
2026-03-18T16:09:24.7455890Z Since `parse_member_type_alias` is a Parser member function, it has access to `struct_parsing_context_stack_` and can call `gSymbolTable.get_current_namespace_handle()` to build the full qualified name, similar to how we handle enums during parsing. I need to apply the same fix we made for enums to the typedef/using code path in `Parser_Decl_TypedefUsing.cpp`. The key insight is that `struct_parsing_context_stack_` already has the current struct pushed when we're parsing members, so I can walk that stack to build the proper qualified name instead of just using the simple struct name.
2026-03-18T16:09:24.7458343Z 
2026-03-18T16:09:24.7461262Z The real issue is that for deeply nested structs, we're not building the full chain correctly—when parsing struct C inside struct B inside struct A in namespace ns, we need to construct the complete qualified path rather than just the immediate context. When parsing C inside B inside A, I'm only using the back of the stack to build the qualified name, which gives me "B::C" instead of the correct "A::B::C". The fix is to iterate through the entire stack from the beginning, concatenating each struct name with the current one to build the full qualified path.
2026-03-18T16:09:24.7463595Z 
2026-03-18T16:09:24.7464113Z Now I need to verify whether `full_qualified_name` is actually used correctly in the namespace case and test if deeply nested structs without enums work as expected.
2026-03-18T16:09:26.5620708Z 
2026-03-18T16:09:26.5621171Z function:
2026-03-18T16:09:26.5621538Z   name: bash

2026-03-18T16:12:43.1345636Z copilot: So:
2026-03-18T16:12:43.1346308Z 1. **3-level struct nesting** (Issue 2): FAILS - "Missing identifier" when parsing `ns::A::B::C`
2026-03-18T16:12:43.1347262Z 2. **typedef/using enum ADL** (Issue 1): FAILS - "Missing variable: Ok" - the enum enumerator `Ok` from `AliasStatus` is not found
2026-03-18T16:12:43.1348005Z 
2026-03-18T16:12:43.1348143Z Now let me understand what needs to be fixed:
2026-03-18T16:12:43.1348361Z 
2026-03-18T16:12:43.1348506Z **Fix for Issue 2: 3-level nested struct name building**
2026-03-18T16:12:43.1348884Z 
2026-03-18T16:12:43.1349331Z The fix at lines 100-116 needs to walk the full context stack:
2026-03-18T16:12:43.1349815Z ```cpp
2026-03-18T16:12:43.1349999Z if (is_nested_class) {
2026-03-18T16:12:43.1350138Z 
2026-03-18T16:12:43.1350141Z 
2026-03-18T16:12:43.1350208Z ```
2026-03-18T16:12:43.1350308Z 
2026-03-18T16:12:43.1350655Z Building the qualified name by iterating through the context stack...
2026-03-18T16:12:43.1350966Z 
2026-03-18T16:12:43.1352340Z But I also need to handle the namespace case for nested structs—if struct A is in namespace `ns` and B is inside A, then B's qualified name should be "A::B", not include the namespace prefix again.
2026-03-18T16:12:43.1353226Z 
2026-03-18T16:12:43.1354496Z The real issue is that when looking up `ns::A::B::C`, the parser traverses through the namespace and nested structs, but the registration only captures the struct chain without the namespace context, so relative lookups inside nested structs might fail to find the correct qualified name.
2026-03-18T16:12:43.1356421Z 
2026-03-18T16:12:43.1356836Z ```
2026-03-18T16:12:43.1357190Z Tracing type resolution...
2026-03-18T16:12:43.1357602Z ```
2026-03-18T16:12:43.1358091Z gTypesByName["B::C"] → TypeInfo for C
2026-03-18T16:12:43.1358699Z gTypesByName["A::B::C"] → NOT FOUND
2026-03-18T16:12:43.1359159Z ```
2026-03-18T16:12:43.1359325Z 
2026-03-18T16:12:43.1361829Z The issue is that nested struct type names are built from just the immediate parent chain, not the full ancestry. When C is registered as "B::C" instead of "A::B::C", the lookup fails. I need to walk the entire context stack to construct the complete qualified name, and then decide whether to include the namespace prefix or keep it separate from the struct nesting chain. So the issue is that B gets registered as "A::B" without the namespace prefix, and then C gets registered as "B::C" - but when we try to look up "ns::A::B::C", neither of those keys exist in the registry. I'm wondering if the two-level case like "ns::A::B" even works correctly, or if there's a fundamental problem with how nested types are being qualified.

2026-03-18T16:12:58.2160639Z

2026-03-18T16:12:58.2161364Z 
2026-03-18T16:12:58.2162250Z Actually, looking at the error location: "test_2level_struct.cpp:9:11" which is `ns::A::B b;` column 11 - that's the `b` (the variable name). So maybe it's actually failing when trying to parse the variable declaration, and the type lookup for `ns::A::B` is failing.
2026-03-18T16:12:58.2163304Z  
2026-03-18T16:12:58.2164419Z The column position is pointing to the `B` in the type name, which means the parser is failing to resolve the nested struct type itself. So the real issue is that 2-level nesting in a namespace isn't working at all - this is a more fundamental problem with how nested struct names are being built and registered.
2026-03-18T16:12:58.2166014Z 
2026-03-18T16:12:58.2166819Z Now I need to trace through how `lookupTypeInCurrentContext` processes qualified names like `ns::A::B` to understand where the lookup is failing.

2026-03-18T16:13:09.1129939Z     path: /home/runner/work/FlashCpp/FlashCpp/src/Parser_Templates_Substitution.cpp
2026-03-18T16:13:09.1130900Z     view_range: |
2026-03-18T16:13:09.1131400Z       [
2026-03-18T16:13:09.1131683Z         1126,

2026-03-18T16:13:09.1132426Z       ]
2026-03-18T16:13:09.1135799Z   result: |
2026-03-18T16:13:09.1137938Z     1126. const TypeInfo* lookupTypeInCurrentContext(StringHandle type_handle) {
2026-03-18T16:13:09.1139004Z     1127. 	// Walk current namespace chain outward first (e.g., physics::Vector, ::Vector)
2026-03-18T16:13:09.1140013Z     1128. 	// This must come before the unqualified lookup so that namespace-local types
2026-03-18T16:13:09.1140939Z     1129. 	// are preferred over short aliases registered by other namespaces.
2026-03-18T16:13:09.1141884Z     1130. 	NamespaceHandle ns_handle = gSymbolTable.get_current_namespace_handle();
2026-03-18T16:13:09.1142609Z     1131. 	while (ns_handle.isValid()) {
2026-03-18T16:13:09.1143693Z     1132. 		StringHandle qualified = gNamespaceRegistry.buildQualifiedIdentifier(ns_handle, type_handle);
2026-03-18T16:13:09.1144328Z     1133. 		auto q_it = gTypesByName.find(qualified);
2026-03-18T16:13:09.1144664Z     1134. 		if (q_it != gTypesByName.end()) {
2026-03-18T16:13:09.1144954Z     1135. 			return q_it->second;
2026-03-18T16:13:09.1145430Z     1136. 		}
2026-03-18T16:13:09.1145638Z     1137. 		if (ns_handle.isGlobal()) {
2026-03-18T16:13:09.1145889Z     1138. 			break;
2026-03-18T16:13:09.1146077Z     1139. 		}
2026-03-18T16:13:09.1146568Z     1140. 		ns_handle = gNamespaceRegistry.getParent(ns_handle);
2026-03-18T16:13:09.1146895Z     1141. 	}
2026-03-18T16:13:09.1147069Z     1142. 
2026-03-18T16:13:09.1147281Z     1143. 	// Fallback: direct unqualified lookup
2026-03-18T16:13:09.1147608Z     1144. 	auto it = gTypesByName.find(type_handle);
2026-03-18T16:13:09.1147915Z     1145. 	if (it != gTypesByName.end()) {
2026-03-18T16:13:09.1148183Z     1146. 		return it->second;
2026-03-18T16:13:09.1148407Z     1147. 	}
2026-03-18T16:13:09.1148580Z     1148. 
2026-03-18T16:13:09.1148756Z     1149. 	// using directives
2026-03-18T16:13:09.1149163Z     1150. 	for (NamespaceHandle using_ns : gSymbolTable.get_current_using_directive_handles()) {
2026-03-18T16:13:09.1149635Z     1151. 		if (!using_ns.isValid()) continue;
2026-03-18T16:13:09.1150151Z     1152. 		StringHandle qualified = gNamespaceRegistry.buildQualifiedIdentifier(using_ns, type_handle);
2026-03-18T16:13:09.1150845Z     1153. 		auto u_it = gTypesByName.find(qualified);
2026-03-18T16:13:09.1151170Z     1154. 		if (u_it != gTypesByName.end()) {
2026-03-18T16:13:09.1151442Z     1155. 			return u_it->second;
2026-03-18T16:13:09.1151673Z     1156. 		}
2026-03-18T16:13:09.1151846Z     1157. 	}
2026-03-18T16:13:09.1152012Z     1158. 
2026-03-18T16:13:09.1152402Z     1159. 	// Fallback: unique suffix match (e.g., std::size_t when current namespace context is unavailable)
2026-03-18T16:13:09.1153018Z     1160. 	std::string_view type_name_sv = StringTable::getStringView(type_handle);
2026-03-18T16:13:09.1153471Z     1161. 	const TypeInfo* suffix_match = nullptr;
2026-03-18T16:13:09.1153824Z     1162. 	for (const auto& [handle, info] : gTypesByName) {
2026-03-18T16:13:09.1154244Z     1163. 		std::string_view full_name = StringTable::getStringView(handle);
2026-03-18T16:13:09.1154694Z     1164. 		if (full_name.size() <= type_name_sv.size() + 2) continue;
2026-03-18T16:13:09.1155206Z     1165. 		if (!full_name.ends_with(type_name_sv)) continue;
2026-03-18T16:13:09.1155636Z     1166. 		size_t prefix_pos = full_name.size() - type_name_sv.size();
2026-03-18T16:13:09.1156177Z     1167. 		if (prefix_pos < 2 || full_name[prefix_pos - 2] != ':' || full_name[prefix_pos - 1] != ':') continue;
2026-03-18T16:13:09.1156672Z     1168. 		if (suffix_match && suffix_match != info) {
2026-03-18T16:13:09.1156989Z     1169. 			// Ambiguous - multiple matches
2026-03-18T16:13:09.1157269Z     1170. 			suffix_match = nullptr;
2026-03-18T16:13:09.1157518Z     1171. 			break;
2026-03-18T16:13:09.1157703Z     1172. 		}
2026-03-18T16:13:09.1157894Z     1173. 		suffix_match = info;
2026-03-18T16:13:09.1158122Z     1174. 	}
2026-03-18T16:13:09.1158312Z     1175. 	if (suffix_match) {
2026-03-18T16:13:09.1158545Z     1176. 		return suffix_match;
2026-03-18T16:13:09.1158767Z     1177. 	}
2026-03-18T16:13:09.1158937Z     1178. 
2026-03-18T16:13:09.1159115Z     1179. 	return nullptr;
2026-03-18T16:13:09.1159327Z     1180. }
2026-03-18T16:13:09.1159496Z     1181. 
2026-03-18T16:13:09.1159888Z     1182. // Expand a PackExpansionExprNode into multiple substituted arguments for function calls.
2026-03-18T16:13:09.1160606Z     1183. // For each pack element, the pattern expression is cloned with the pack identifier replaced,
2026-03-18T16:13:09.1161124Z     1184. // then template parameters are substituted.
2026-03-18T16:13:09.1161468Z     1185. bool Parser::expandPackExpansionArgs(
2026-03-18T16:13:09.1161809Z     1186. 	const PackExpansionExprNode& pack_expansion,
2026-03-18T16:13:09.1162170Z     1187. 	const InlineVector<ASTNode, 4>& template_params,
2026-03-18T16:13:09.1162556Z     1188. 	const InlineVector<TemplateTypeArg, 4>& template_args,
2026-03-18T16:13:09.1162914Z     1189. 	ChunkedVector<ASTNode>& out_args) {
2026-03-18T16:13:09.1163177Z     1190. 
2026-03-18T16:13:09.1163419Z     1191. 	const ASTNode& pattern = pack_expansion.pattern();
2026-03-18T16:13:09.1163724Z     1192. 
2026-03-18T16:13:09.1164018Z     1193. 	// Find the variadic template parameter and count non-variadic params
2026-03-18T16:13:09.1164563Z     1194. 	size_t variadic_param_idx = SIZE_MAX;
2026-03-18T16:13:09.1164866Z     1195. 	size_t non_variadic_count = 0;
2026-03-18T16:13:09.1165328Z     1196. 	for (size_t p = 0; p < template_params.size(); ++p) {
2026-03-18T16:13:09.1165733Z     1197. 		if (template_params[p].is<TemplateParameterNode>()) {
2026-03-18T16:13:09.1166183Z     1198. 			const auto& tparam = template_params[p].as<TemplateParameterNode>();
2026-03-18T16:13:09.1166634Z     1199. 			if (tparam.is_variadic()) variadic_param_idx = p;
2026-03-18T16:13:09.1166963Z     1200. 			else non_variadic_count++;
2026-03-18T16:13:26.1200977Z 
2026-03-18T16:13:26.1201641Z copilot: I see. So `lookupTypeInCurrentContext("A::B")`:
2026-03-18T16:13:26.1202763Z 1. First tries `ns::A::B` → builds qualified identifier from current namespace + "A::B"
2026-03-18T16:13:26.1203305Z 2. If not found, tries direct "A::B"
2026-03-18T16:13:26.1203644Z 3. If not found, tries using directives  
2026-03-18T16:13:26.1203991Z 4. If not found, tries suffix match
2026-03-18T16:13:26.1204470Z 
2026-03-18T16:13:26.1205747Z When parsing `ns::A::B`, the type handle would be "A::B" (after stripping the namespace prefix `ns::`). Then `lookupTypeInCurrentContext("A::B")` would:1. Try `ns::A::B` → NOT found (because B is registered as "A::B" only)
2026-03-18T16:13:26.1206676Z 
2026-03-18T16:13:26.1206679Z 
2026-03-18T16:13:26.1206959Z 2. Try direct "A::B" → FOUND (if B was correctly registered as "A::B")
2026-03-18T16:13:26.1207282Z 
2026-03-18T16:13:26.1208546Z So for 2-level nesting, the direct lookup should work. But the test is failing, which means the real problem might be in how the parser tokenizes `ns::A::B b;` — it's probably treating `ns`, `::`, `A`, `::`, `B` as separate tokens rather than building the full qualified name correctly.

2026-03-18T16:13:59.2660397Z copilot: I see. The restore points ("RESTORING TO 'ns' TOKEN") indicate that parsing `ns::A::B b;` as a type specifier first tried to parse it as a function declaration (with `parse_function_declaration`), which failed, then tried again as a declaration. It seems it got confused about whether `ns::A::B b;` is a type declaration or something else.
2026-03-18T16:13:59.2662462Z 
2026-03-18T16:13:59.2664187Z But the key issue is: when parsing `ns::A::B`, what happens at the type specifier level? The parser sees `ns::A::B` and:1. Recognizes `ns` as a namespace identifier2. Follows `::A` → looks up `A` in namespace `ns`3. 
2026-03-18T16:13:59.2665701Z 
2026-03-18T16:13:59.2667712Z Looking at the trace, the parser starts at `ns` but backs up when it hits `::`, which suggests the initial attempt to parse this as a type or expression failed at the scope resolution operator. The problem seems to be that nested type lookup isn't properly resolving `B` as a member type within `A`.
2026-03-18T16:13:59.2669126Z 
2026-03-18T16:13:59.2670193Z Now I'm examining what's actually registered in the type registry—there's both a simple `B` entry and a qualified `A::B` entry, so when the parser tries to look up the type, it should find the qualified version under the namespace context.
2026-03-18T16:13:59.2671052Z 
2026-03-18T16:13:59.2672065Z The real issue might be in how the type specifier parsing handles nested qualified names like `ns::A::B`—it could be treating this as a member type lookup rather than a direct string match, which would fail if the nested type resolution logic isn't working correctly.

2026-03-18T16:16:10.0698417Z 
2026-03-18T16:16:10.0699175Z copilot: Let me trace the exact registration path for nested structs in namespaces:
2026-03-18T16:16:10.0701290Z 
2026-03-18T16:16:10.0701791Z copilot: At line 1316-1320, after parsing a nested struct, the code builds:
2026-03-18T16:16:10.0702877Z ```cpp
2026-03-18T16:16:10.0703198Z std::string_view qualified_nested_name = StringBuilder()
2026-03-18T16:16:10.0703731Z     .append(qualified_struct_name)
2026-03-18T16:16:10.0704071Z     .append("::")
2026-03-18T16:16:10.0704341Z     .append(nested_struct.name())
2026-03-18T16:16:10.0704762Z     .commit();
2026-03-18T16:16:10.0704978Z ```
2026-03-18T16:16:10.0705342Z 
2026-03-18T16:16:10.0706254Z So `qualified_nested_name` uses `qualified_struct_name` which was already set at parse time. Let me trace through for 3 levels:- Parsing A in ns:  - `qualified_struct_name` = "ns::A" (from full_qualified_name line 113-115)
2026-03-18T16:16:10.0707218Z 
2026-03-18T16:16:10.0707222Z 
2026-03-18T16:16:10.0709135Z - After B is parsed inside A, `qualified_nested_name` becomes "ns::A::B" and gets registered in gTypesByName, but B itself was already added during its own parsing as "A::B" since `is_nested_class` was true and `qualified_struct_name` was just "A::B" at that point. Now I'm seeing the potential issue: B gets registered twice with different qualified names depending on when and how it's accessed.
2026-03-18T16:16:10.0710844Z 
2026-03-18T16:16:10.0714525Z Looking at the debug trace, I see A being registered as "ns::A" but I'm not seeing B's registration logged, which is suspicious. Let me trace through what happens at the point where the outer struct registers nested names to understand why the test is failing despite "ns::A::B" appearing to be registered correctly. I see the bug now—when the nested struct B gets registered during parsing, it's stored under the key "A::B", but later when the outer struct A tries to look it up using the fully qualified name "ns::A::B", the lookup fails because there's a mismatch between how it was registered and how we're searching for it. This means the StructTypeInfo for A never gets updated with the nested type information.
2026-03-18T16:16:10.0717476Z 
2026-03-18T16:16:10.0718782Z The real question is how the parser actually resolves a qualified name like `ns::A::B` when it encounters it as a type declaration—it probably walks through the namespace hierarchy first to find `ns::A`, then looks for `B` as a nested type within struct A. Or it might build the full string "ns::A::B" and look it up in gTypesByName.
2026-03-18T16:16:10.0720160Z 
2026-03-18T16:16:10.0721222Z Looking at the trace, I see two restore points at the `ns` token, which means the parser tried different interpretations of `ns::A::B b;` and both failed at different points. This suggests the type specifier parsing is trying to construct the qualified name but something's going wrong with how nested types are registered.
2026-03-18T16:16:10.0722359Z 
2026-03-18T16:16:10.0724048Z The real issue is that when we parse nested struct B inside ns::A, we're storing `type_name` as just "A::B" instead of the fully qualified "ns::A::B", and the same problem cascades for deeper nesting. I need to either fix how we compute the type name to include the namespace, or register aliases for all the lookup paths we might need—similar to what we already do for enums.
2026-03-18T16:16:10.0726504Z 
2026-03-18T16:16:10.0726644Z function:
2026-03-18T16:16:10.0726836Z   name: view

2026-03-18T16:16:10.0729113Z     1305. 						}

2026-03-18T16:16:10.0729668Z     1307. 						if (auto nested_node = nested_result.node()) {

2026-03-18T16:16:10.0734283Z     1317. 								.append(qualified_struct_name)
2026-03-18T16:16:10.0734575Z     1318. 								.append("::")
2026-03-18T16:16:10.0734975Z     1319. 								.append(nested_struct.name())
2026-03-18T16:16:10.0735568Z     1320. 								.commit();
2026-03-18T16:16:10.0736220Z     1321. 							auto nested_type_it = gTypesByName.find(StringTable::getOrInternStringHandle(qualified_nested_name));
2026-03-18T16:16:10.0736795Z     1322. 							if (nested_type_it != gTypesByName.end()) {
2026-03-18T16:16:10.0737521Z     1323. 								StructTypeInfo* nested_info = nested_type_it->second->getStructInfo();
2026-03-18T16:16:10.0737965Z     1324. 								if (nested_info) {
2026-03-18T16:16:10.0738263Z     1325. 									struct_info->addNestedClass(nested_info);
2026-03-18T16:16:10.0738562Z     1326. 								}
2026-03-18T16:16:10.0738748Z     1327. 
2026-03-18T16:16:10.0739115Z     1328. 								auto qualified_name = StringTable::getOrInternStringHandle(qualified_nested_name);
2026-03-18T16:16:10.0739834Z     1329. 								if (gTypesByName.find(qualified_name) == gTypesByName.end()) {
2026-03-18T16:16:10.0740317Z     1330. 									gTypesByName.emplace(qualified_name, nested_type_it->second);
2026-03-18T16:16:10.0740673Z     1331. 								}
2026-03-18T16:16:10.0740854Z     1332. 							}
2026-03-18T16:16:10.0741037Z     1333. 							
2026-03-18T16:16:10.0741355Z     1334. 							// Handle any variable declarators parsed after the nested declaration
2026-03-18T16:16:10.0741864Z     1335. 							// e.g., "union Data { ... } data;" - the "data" member should be added
2026-03-18T16:16:10.0742301Z     1336. 							for (auto& var_node : pending_struct_variables_) {
2026-03-18T16:16:10.0742761Z     1337. 								// Extract the declaration node from the VariableDeclarationNode wrapper
2026-03-18T16:16:10.0743279Z     1338. 								auto& var_decl_node = var_node.as<VariableDeclarationNode>();
2026-03-18T16:16:10.0743873Z     1339. 								auto decl_node = var_decl_node.declaration_node();
2026-03-18T16:16:10.0744187Z     1340. 								
2026-03-18T16:16:10.0744434Z     1341. 								// Add as a member of the outer struct
2026-03-18T16:16:10.0744833Z     1342. 								struct_ref.add_member(decl_node, current_access, std::nullopt);
2026-03-18T16:16:10.0745318Z     1343. 							}
2026-03-18T16:16:10.0745537Z     1344. 							pending_struct_variables_.clear();
2026-03-18T16:16:10.0745814Z     1345. 						}
2026-03-18T16:16:10.0745990Z     1346. 
2026-03-18T16:16:10.0746187Z     1347. 						continue;  // Skip to next member
2026-03-18T16:16:10.0746467Z     1348. 					} else {
2026-03-18T16:16:10.0746835Z     1349. 						// Pattern 3: Member with struct type (struct Name member; or struct Name *ptr;)
2026-03-18T16:16:10.0747317Z     1350. 						// Restore and let normal member parsing handle it
2026-03-18T16:16:10.0747669Z     1351. 						restore_token_position(saved_pos);
2026-03-18T16:16:10.0747938Z     1352. 					}
2026-03-18T16:16:10.0748124Z     1353. 				} else {
2026-03-18T16:16:10.0748442Z     1354. 					// Not a nested declaration, restore and let normal parsing handle it
2026-03-18T16:16:10.0748849Z     1355. 					restore_token_position(saved_pos);
2026-03-18T16:16:10.0749116Z     1356. 				}
2026-03-18T16:16:10.0749290Z     1357. 			}
2026-03-18T16:16:10.0749464Z     1358. 		}
2026-03-18T16:16:10.0749631Z     1359. 
2026-03-18T16:16:10.0750087Z     1360. 		// Check for constexpr, consteval, inline, explicit specifiers (can appear on constructors and member functions)
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0106547Z 
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0107188Z copilot: Now I have a complete picture. Let me plan the fixes:
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0107987Z 
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0108706Z 1. **Issue 2 (nested struct name - 3+ levels)**: Fix `type_name` building at lines 100-116 to walk the full context stack AND include namespace.
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0110232Z 2. **Issue 1 (typedef/using enum aliases)**: Fix `parse_member_type_alias` to also register "ns::Container::AliasStatus".
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0111429Z 3. **Issue 3 (no-mangled-name dedup)**: Use function source name as fallback identity key.
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0112030Z 
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0112363Z Let me start with the most impactful fix - the nested struct name building:
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0112895Z 
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0113088Z copilot: I see the problem more clearly now.
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0113427Z 
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0113563Z At lines 1316-1321:
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0113877Z ```cpp
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0114278Z std::string_view qualified_nested_name = StringBuilder()
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0115522Z     .append(qualified_struct_name)  // "ns::A" for a top-level struct in ns
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0116230Z     .append("::")
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0116685Z     .append(nested_struct.name())   // "B" (simple name)
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0117340Z     .commit();                        // = "ns::A::B"
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0118222Z auto nested_type_it = gTypesByName.find(StringTable::getOrInternStringHandle(qualified_nested_name));
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0119075Z ```
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0119240Z 
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0119625Z So `qualified_nested_name` = "ns::A::B" for the case where A is in namespace ns.
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0120173Z 
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0120178Z 
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0122312Z The issue is that nested struct B was registered with `type_name = "A::B"` instead of the fully qualified `"ns::A::B"`, so the lookup at line 1321 fails. The fix is to ensure the nested struct's type name includes the namespace prefix when it's registered, so both the registration and lookup use the same fully qualified name. This same pattern would apply recursively for deeper nesting levels.
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0124762Z 
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0126524Z The root problem is that `type_name` for nested classes only captures one parent level, but it should walk the entire context stack and prepend the namespace to build the complete qualified name.
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0127793Z 
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0128075Z Building the fix... Building the qualified name logic...
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0128515Z 
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0130824Z Now I'm checking whether including the namespace in the type_name for nested structs causes issues—when a nested struct B gets registered with its full qualified name like "ns::A::B", it needs to be findable both by that full path and by its simple name "B" in the type registry.
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0132568Z 
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0134305Z Looking at the registration logic, the simple name gets registered for nested classes, but the intermediate chain "A::B" wouldn't be registered anymore if we switch to the full namespace-qualified version, which could break existing lookups that depend on that path working as a fallback.
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0136413Z 
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0139235Z So the fix needs three parts: first, update nested struct type names to include the full namespace-qualified chain while also registering the struct-chain-only version as a fallback; second, do the same for type aliases registered in `parse_member_type_alias` so they're accessible via their fully-qualified names; and third, handle the no-mangled-name dedup case by using the function name handle as a fallback identity since function names are also interned strings.
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0142261Z 
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0142739Z Let me check what other fields FunctionDeclarationNode exposes besides mangled_name...
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0143508Z 
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0143952Z function:
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0144276Z   name: view

2026-03-18T16:16:40.0145687Z     path: /home/runner/work/FlashCpp/FlashCpp/src/Parser_Decl_StructEnum.cpp
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0146411Z     view_range: |
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0148533Z       [
