2026-03-18T16:16:40.0167012Z     95. 	// - For nested classes: Parent::Child
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0167546Z     96. 	// - For namespace classes: ns::Class  
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0168105Z     97. 	// - For top-level classes: just the simple name
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0168654Z     98. 	StringHandle full_qualified_name;
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0169095Z     99. 	
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0169402Z     100. 	if (is_nested_class) {
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0169909Z     101. 		// We're inside a struct, so this is a nested class
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0170652Z     102. 		// Use the qualified name (e.g., "Outer::Inner") for the TypeInfo entry
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0171478Z     103. 		const auto& context = struct_parsing_context_stack_.back();
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0172301Z     104. 		// Build the qualified name using StringBuilder for a persistent allocation
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0173351Z     105. 		qualified_struct_name = StringTable::getOrInternStringHandle(StringBuilder()
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0174026Z     106. 			.append(context.struct_name)
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0174304Z     107. 			.append("::")
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0174541Z     108. 			.append(struct_name));
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0174813Z     109. 		type_name = qualified_struct_name;
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0175485Z     110. 		full_qualified_name = qualified_struct_name;
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0175835Z     111. 	} else if (!qualified_namespace.empty()) {
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0176308Z     112. 		// Top-level class in a namespace - use namespace-qualified name for proper mangling
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0177289Z     113. 		full_qualified_name = gNamespaceRegistry.buildQualifiedIdentifier(current_namespace_handle, struct_name);
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0178661Z     114. 		qualified_struct_name = full_qualified_name;  // Also update qualified_struct_name for implicit constructors
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0179794Z     115. 		type_name = full_qualified_name;  // TypeInfo should also use fully qualified name
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0180456Z     116. 	}
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0180721Z     117. 
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0181262Z     118. 	TypeInfo& struct_type_info = add_struct_type(type_name, current_namespace_handle);
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0181929Z     119. 
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0182456Z     120. 	// For nested classes, also register with the simple name so it can be referenced
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0183308Z     121. 	// from within the nested class itself (e.g., in constructors)
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0183876Z     122. 	if (is_nested_class) {
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0184192Z     123. 		gTypesByName.emplace(struct_name, &struct_type_info);
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0184515Z     124. 	}
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0184686Z     125. 	
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0185318Z     126. 	// For namespace classes, also register with the simple name for 'this' pointer lookup
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0186224Z     127. 	// during member function code generation. The TypeInfo's name is fully qualified (ns::Test)
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0186819Z     128. 	// but parent_struct_name is just "Test", so we need this alias for lookups.
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0187295Z     129. 	if (!is_nested_class && !qualified_namespace.empty()) {
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0187708Z     130. 		if (gTypesByName.find(struct_name) == gTypesByName.end()) {
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0188129Z     131. 			gTypesByName.emplace(struct_name, &struct_type_info);
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0188446Z     132. 		}
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0188619Z     133. 	}
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0188785Z     134. 
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0189135Z     135. 	// If inside an inline namespace, register the parent-qualified name (e.g., outer::Foo)
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0190023Z     136. 	if (!qualified_namespace.empty() && !inline_namespace_stack_.empty() && inline_namespace_stack_.back() && !parsing_template_class_) {
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0190873Z     137. 		NamespaceHandle parent_namespace_handle = gNamespaceRegistry.getParent(current_namespace_handle);
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0191699Z     138. 		StringHandle parent_handle = gNamespaceRegistry.buildQualifiedIdentifier(parent_namespace_handle, struct_name);
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0192387Z     139. 		if (gTypesByName.find(parent_handle) == gTypesByName.end()) {
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0192833Z     140. 			gTypesByName.emplace(parent_handle, &struct_type_info);
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0193164Z     141. 		}
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0193342Z     142. 	}
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0193508Z     143. 	
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0193840Z     144. 	// Register with namespace-qualified names for all levels of the namespace path
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0194905Z     145. 	// This allows lookups like "inner::Base" when we're in namespace "ns" to find "ns::inner::Base"
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0195683Z     146. 	if (!qualified_namespace.empty() && !is_nested_class) {
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0196125Z     147. 		// full_qualified_name already computed above, just log if needed
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0196692Z     148. 		FLASH_LOG(Parser, Debug, "Registered struct '", StringTable::getStringView(struct_name), 
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0197364Z     149. 		          "' with namespace-qualified name '", StringTable::getStringView(full_qualified_name), "'");
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0197826Z     150. 		
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0198157Z     151. 		// Also register intermediate names (e.g., "inner::Base" for "ns::inner::Base")
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0198640Z     152. 		// This allows sibling namespace access patterns like:
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0199164Z     153. 		// namespace ns { namespace inner { struct Base {}; } struct Derived : public inner::Base {}; }
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0199912Z     154. 		for (size_t pos = qualified_namespace.find("::"); pos != std::string_view::npos; pos = qualified_namespace.find("::", pos + 2)) {
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0200559Z     155. 			std::string_view suffix = qualified_namespace.substr(pos + 2);
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0200942Z     156. 			StringBuilder partial_qualified;
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0201333Z     157. 			partial_qualified.append(suffix).append("::").append(struct_name);
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0201801Z     158. 			std::string_view partial_view = partial_qualified.commit();
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0202286Z     159. 			auto partial_handle = StringTable::getOrInternStringHandle(partial_view);
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0202792Z     160. 			if (gTypesByName.find(partial_handle) == gTypesByName.end()) {
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0203245Z     161. 				gTypesByName.emplace(partial_handle, &struct_type_info);
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0203770Z     162. 				FLASH_LOG(Parser, Debug, "Registered struct '", StringTable::getStringView(struct_name), 
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0204299Z     163. 				          "' with partial qualified name '", partial_view, "'");
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0204619Z     164. 			}
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0204792Z     165. 		}
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0204960Z     166. 	}
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0205230Z     167. 
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0205543Z     168. 	// Check for alignas specifier after struct name (if not already specified)
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0206116Z     169. 	if (!custom_alignment.has_value()) {
2026-03-18T16:16:40.0206443Z     170. 		custom_alignment = parse_alignas_specifier();
2026-03-18T16:17:05.1758101Z 
2026-03-18T16:17:05.1758908Z copilot: OK, now I have a clear understanding. The fix for the nested struct name building:
2026-03-18T16:17:05.1759499Z 
2026-03-18T16:17:05.1759613Z The key is that when is_nested_class:
2026-03-18T16:17:05.1760092Z - We build the struct chain by walking the full context stack
2026-03-18T16:17:05.1760745Z - We also need to prepend the namespace (if any) to get the full qualified name
2026-03-18T16:17:05.1761283Z - The `type_name` should be the FULL ns-qualified name (e.g., "ns::A::B::C")
2026-03-18T16:17:05.1761807Z - We also need to register the struct-chain-only name (e.g., "A::B::C") as a fallback
2026-03-18T16:17:05.1762144Z 
2026-03-18T16:17:05.1762232Z Here's my plan:
2026-03-18T16:17:05.1762354Z 
2026-03-18T16:17:05.1762688Z ```cpp
2026-03-18T16:17:05.1762870Z if (is_nested_class) {
2026-03-18T16:17:05.1763005Z 
2026-03-18T16:17:05.1763008Z 
2026-03-18T16:17:05.1763081Z ```
2026-03-18T16:17:05.1763171Z 
2026-03-18T16:17:05.1763362Z Building the struct chain by iterating through the context stack...
2026-03-18T16:17:05.1763651Z 
2026-03-18T16:17:05.1763758Z Handling the qualified name construction...
2026-03-18T16:17:05.1763984Z 
2026-03-18T16:17:05.1764963Z Now I'm considering whether we need to register the struct chain alias separately when a namespace is present. If someone looks up just "A::B::C" without the namespace prefix, the lookup should still find the fully qualified "ns::A::B::C" version that's registered as the type name.
2026-03-18T16:17:05.1766394Z 
2026-03-18T16:17:05.1766477Z ```
2026-03-18T16:17:05.1766580Z 
2026-03-18T16:17:05.1766651Z cpp
2026-03-18T16:17:05.1766901Z std::string_view qualified_nested_name = StringBuilder()
2026-03-18T16:17:05.1767252Z     .append(qualified_struct
2026-03-18T16:17:05.1767500Z ```
2026-03-18T16:17:05.1767599Z 
2026-03-18T16:17:05.1768119Z Building the qualified nested name... `gTypesByName.find("ns::A::B::C")` → FOUND.
2026-03-18T16:17:05.1768470Z 
2026-03-18T16:17:05.1772231Z For the struct-only chain case, I need to verify that the intermediate `qualified_struct_name` values used when building `qualified_nested_name` match what actually gets registered. The context stack stores simple names, so when I walk through nested structs C inside B inside A within a namespace, I build the qualified path step by step—and after applying the fix to how B's qualified name is computed, the chain should resolve correctly all the way down. I need to trace through what happens when `type_name` changes from the simple "A::B" to the fully qualified "ns::A::B". The main concern is whether this breaks anything in `add_struct_type`, the TypeInfo's name field, member function mangling, or parent struct references in function declarations. Let me check what `add_struct_type` actually does with that parameter.

2026-03-18T16:18:12.6335962Z

2026-03-18T16:18:12.6339674Z 
2026-03-18T16:18:12.6339795Z Building the qualified name for nested structs...
2026-03-18T16:18:12.6340012Z 
2026-03-18T16:18:12.6341890Z After our fix for B: B is registered as "ns::A::B". And `qualified_nested_name` = "ns::A" + "::" + "B" = "ns::A::B". So the lookup `gTypesByName.find("ns::A::B")` → FOUND! Great. For C inside B inside A (in ns), the same pattern applies—we'd construct "ns::A::B::C" and find it in the registry.
2026-03-18T16:18:12.6343960Z 
2026-03-18T16:18:12.6344678Z Now I'm thinking about whether we also need to register the struct_chain-only name as an alias. When code within namespace `ns` uses `A::B c;`, the lookup for "A::B" would try `ns::A::B` and find it. But what about `A::B::C c;`?
2026-03-18T16:18:12.6345693Z 
2026-03-18T16:18:12.6346976Z The lookup for "A::B::C" would walk the namespace chain and try `ns::A::B::C`, which we've already registered as the full type name. So we don't need to additionally register "A::B::C" as a bare alias since `lookupTypeInCurrentContext` automatically prepends the namespace. But now I need to check what `FunctionDeclarationNode::parent_struct_name_` is used for and how it gets set.

2026-03-18T16:18:45.9828479Z copilot: I see that `parent_struct_name()` is used extensively in codegen for `gTypesByName.find(...)` lookups. If we change `qualified_struct_name` (which is used to set `parent_struct_name`) to include the full namespace ("ns::A::B"), then those lookups will work correctly since we're registering the struct as "ns::A::B" in gTypesByName.
2026-03-18T16:18:45.9830017Z 
2026-03-18T16:18:45.9830194Z Let me check line 358 in IrGenerator_Visitors_Decl.cpp:
2026-03-18T16:18:45.9830567Z ```cpp
2026-03-18T16:18:45.9831053Z auto type_it = gTypesByName.find(StringTable::getOrInternStringHandle(node.parent_struct_name()));
2026-03-18T16:18:45.9831550Z 
2026-03-18T16:18:45.9831553Z 
2026-03-18T16:18:45.9831634Z ```
2026-03-18T16:18:45.9831749Z 
2026-03-18T16:18:45.9833148Z So if `parent_struct_name()` returns the fully qualified name "ns::A::B", the lookup would succeed as long as we're registering that same fully qualified name. But I'm noticing we also register the simple name "B" at line 123, which means we might be storing both versions in gTypesByName. The question is where `parent_struct_name()` actually gets set from.
2026-03-18T16:18:45.9834661Z 
2026-03-18T16:18:45.9836442Z Looking at the FunctionDeclarationNode constructor, it takes a `parent_struct_name_handle` and stores a string view of it. That handle comes from `qualified_struct_name` when we create the node at line 2057. So after our fix, `parent_struct_name()` would return the full qualified name like "ns::A::B". Then when we try to find it in gTypesByName at line 358, we'd be looking up "ns::A::B" directly.
2026-03-18T16:18:45.9838165Z 
2026-03-18T16:18:45.9838242Z ```
2026-03-18T16:18:45.9838341Z 
2026-03-18T16:18:45.9838407Z cpp
2026-03-18T16:18:45.9838595Z gTypesByName.find("ns::A::B")
2026-03-18T16:18:45.9838939Z ```
2026-03-18T16:18:45.9839106Z 
2026-03-18T16:18:45.9840426Z This should find the type since we registered it with that full qualified name. The fix looks solid. Now I need to trace through what happens with the `struct_info_name` variable to make sure there aren't any other issues downstream.
2026-03-18T16:18:45.9886380Z

2026-03-18T16:19:12.9094593Z 
2026-03-18T16:19:12.9095695Z copilot: Let me look at how qualified types like `ns::A::B` are resolved during type specifier parsing:
2026-03-18T16:19:12.9099463Z 
2026-03-18T16:19:12.9101391Z copilot: Good. `struct_info_name` is used to create the `StructTypeInfo`, which is used for mangling. After our fix, `qualified_struct_name` for nested classes includes the full namespace, so `struct_info_name` = "ns::A::B" which is correct for mangling.
2026-03-18T16:19:12.9103051Z 
2026-03-18T16:19:12.9103243Z Now let me plan the actual code changes:
2026-03-18T16:19:12.9103592Z 
2026-03-18T16:19:12.9103822Z **Fix 1: Nested struct name building** (lines 100-116)
2026-03-18T16:19:12.9104212Z 
2026-03-18T16:19:12.9104333Z ```cpp
2026-03-18T16:19:12.9104640Z if (is_nested_class) {
2026-03-18T16:19:12.9105464Z     // Build the full struct chain by walking the context stack from outermost to innermost.
2026-03-18T16:19:12.9106475Z     // e.g., for struct C inside struct B inside struct A: chain = "A::B::C"
2026-03-18T16:19:12.9106982Z 
2026-03-18T16:19:12.9106988Z 
2026-03-18T16:19:12.9107113Z ```
2026-03-18T16:19:12.9107275Z 
2026-03-18T16:19:12.9107382Z Building the struct chain...
2026-03-18T16:19:12.9107558Z 
2026-03-18T16:19:12.9107713Z ```
2026-03-18T16:19:12.9107864Z 
2026-03-18T16:19:12.9109423Z Wait, I'm realizing there's a problem with forward declarations—if I change how nested structs are named from "A::B" to "ns::A::B" when they're in a namespace, any forward declarations that used the old naming scheme won't match up when I try to look them up later.
2026-03-18T16:19:12.9110532Z 
2026-03-18T16:19:12.9112362Z Actually, forward declarations for nested structs are pretty rare, so I'll skip worrying about that edge case for now. What I do need to handle is registering an alias for the struct chain without the namespace prefix, so code inside the namespace can reference it using just "A::B::C" without needing the full qualified name. I'm adding logic to check if this alias doesn't already exist in the type registry, then storing a reference to the struct type under that shorter name.
2026-03-18T16:19:12.9114372Z 
2026-03-18T16:19:12.9115395Z But now I'm second-guessing whether this is even necessary—when the parser encounters "A::B::C" from within namespace `ns`, it probably already handles the resolution correctly.
2026-03-18T16:19:12.9116242Z 
2026-03-18T16:19:12.9116315Z ```
2026-03-18T16:19:12.9116428Z 
2026-03-18T16:19:12.9116495Z cpp
2026-03-18T16:19:12.9116915Z // Also register struct-chain-only alias for reference within enclosing namespace
2026-03-18T16:19:12.9117449Z // e.g., "A::B::C" so code inside namespace ns can use A::B::C
2026-03-18T16:19:12.9118012Z if (!qualified_namespace.empty() && struct_chain != full_qualified_name) {
2026-03-18T16:19:12.9118564Z     if (gTypesByName.find(struct_chain) == gTypesByName.end()) {
2026-03-18T16:19:12.9119010Z         gTypesByName.emplace(struct_chain, &struct_type_info);
2026-03-18T16:19:12.9119431Z     }
2026-03-18T16:19:12.9119605Z }
2026-03-18T16:19:12.9119759Z ```
2026-03-18T16:19:12.9119854Z 
2026-03-18T16:19:12.9121109Z Actually, I think the real question is how the parser handles the `::` chain when someone writes `ns::A::B::C` from outside the namespace. I need to trace through the type specifier parsing logic to understand whether it's looking up "ns::A" first, then finding B as a nested type of A, and so on. Let me dig into that.
2026-03-18T16:19:12.9122641Z 
2026-03-18T16:19:12.9124047Z But actually, maybe I should just test whether the fix works empirically rather than getting lost in the parsing details. Looking at the error trace, I see the parser is trying to parse `ns::A::B b;` as a function declaration and failing at the `::`, then restoring and trying again. The key is understanding how `parse_type_specifier` resolves qualified types like `ns::A::B`.

2026-03-18T16:19:21.2424551Z     668. 				}

2026-03-18T16:19:21.2426171Z     673. 			} else {

2026-03-18T16:19:21.2427707Z     676. 				type_size = 0;
2026-03-18T16:19:21.2427929Z     677. 			}

2026-03-18T16:19:38.3882994Z 
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3884541Z copilot: This is the struct type specifier path (after `struct` keyword). But for accessing `ns::A::B c;` (without `struct` keyword), it goes through a different path. Let me look at the regular type specifier path for identifier-based types.
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3885756Z 
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3886827Z Actually, looking at the code at line 656: `auto type_it = gTypesByName.find(type_name_handle)`. Here `type_name_handle` is built from the raw tokens, e.g. "ns::A::B". But `ns::A::B` is NOT in gTypesByName (only "A::B" is registered for nested structs without namespace).
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3888046Z  
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3889626Z The fallback lookup tries prepending the current namespace to get `ns::ns::A::B`, which obviously won't exist, and then tries the direct lookup of `ns::A::B` which also fails. So I need to trace through the non-struct-keyword type specifier path to see where the actual resolution happens.
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3901723Z

2026-03-18T16:19:38.3912972Z     704. 				va_list_token, cv_qualifier));
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3913418Z     705. 		}
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3913621Z     706. 		// Fallback: treat as void*
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3914238Z     707. 		TypeSpecifierNode va_list_type(Type::Void, TypeQualifier::None, 0, va_list_token, cv_qualifier);
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3914761Z     708. 		va_list_type.add_pointer_level();

2026-03-18T16:19:38.3916032Z     710. 	}
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3916281Z     711. 	else if (peek() == "::"_tok) {
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3916920Z     712. 		// Handle global-scope-qualified types like ::__gnu_debug::_Safe_iterator
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3917402Z     713. 		// The '::' prefix denotes a type from the global namespace
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3917751Z     714. 		advance();  // consume '::'
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3917989Z     715. 
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3918176Z     716. 		if (!peek().is_identifier()) {
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3918684Z     717. 			return ParseResult::error("Expected identifier after '::'", peek().is_eof() ? Token() : peek_info());
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3919187Z     718. 		}
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3919358Z     719. 
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3919601Z     720. 		// Build qualified name WITHOUT the leading :: prefix.
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3920100Z     721. 		// The :: is just a scope resolution indicator meaning "start from the global namespace"
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3920592Z     722. 		// and should not be part of the stored type name.
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3920934Z     723. 		StringBuilder type_name_builder;
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3921263Z     724. 		type_name_builder.append(peek_info().value());
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3921596Z     725. 		Token type_name_token = peek_info();
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3921870Z     726. 		advance();
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3922067Z     727. 
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3922367Z     728. 		// Continue building qualified name (e.g., __gnu_debug::_Safe_iterator)
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3922759Z     729. 		while (peek() == "::"_tok) {
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3923227Z     730. 			advance();  // consume '::'
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3923488Z     731. 			if (peek() == "template"_tok) {
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3923770Z     732. 				advance();  // consume 'template'
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3924031Z     733. 			}
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3924226Z     734. 			if (!peek().is_identifier()) {
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3924491Z     735. 				type_name_builder.reset();
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3924976Z     736. 				return ParseResult::error("Expected identifier after '::'", peek().is_eof() ? Token() : peek_info());
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3925766Z     737. 			}
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3926053Z     738. 			type_name_builder.append("::"sv).append(peek_info().value());
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3926413Z     739. 			advance();
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3926611Z     740. 		}
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3926786Z     741. 
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3927042Z     742. 		std::string_view type_name = type_name_builder.commit();
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3927362Z     743. 
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3927850Z     744. 		// Update the token to carry the full qualified name (e.g., "__gnu_debug::_Safe_iterator")
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3928612Z     745. 		// so that downstream consumers like FriendDeclarationNode get the complete type name.
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3929144Z     746. 		type_name_token = Token(Token::Type::Identifier, type_name,
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3929633Z     747. 			type_name_token.line(), type_name_token.column(), type_name_token.file_index());
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3930037Z     748. 
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3930391Z     749. 		// Skip template arguments if present (e.g., __gnu_debug::_Safe_iterator<_Ite, _Seq, Tag>)
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3930834Z     750. 		if (peek() == "<"_tok) {
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3931088Z     751. 			skip_template_arguments();
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3931332Z     752. 		}
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3931503Z     753. 
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3931683Z     754. 		// Trailing CV-qualifiers
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3932002Z     755. 		while (peek() == "const"_tok || peek() == "volatile"_tok) {
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3932338Z     756. 			if (peek() == "const"_tok) {
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3932649Z     757. 				cv_qualifier = static_cast<CVQualifier>(
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3933098Z     758. 					static_cast<uint8_t>(cv_qualifier) | static_cast<uint8_t>(CVQualifier::Const));
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3933500Z     759. 			} else {
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3933736Z     760. 				cv_qualifier = static_cast<CVQualifier>(
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3934181Z     761. 					static_cast<uint8_t>(cv_qualifier) | static_cast<uint8_t>(CVQualifier::Volatile));
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3934591Z     762. 			}
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3934771Z     763. 			advance();
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3934964Z     764. 		}
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3935436Z     765. 
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3935657Z     766. 		// Look up the type by its unqualified name
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3936130Z     767. 		StringHandle type_name_handle = StringTable::getOrInternStringHandle(type_name);
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3936632Z     768. 		auto type_it = gTypesByName.find(type_name_handle);
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3936977Z     769. 		if (type_it != gTypesByName.end()) {
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3937314Z     770. 			const TypeInfo* type_info = type_it->second;
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3937676Z     771. 			TypeIndex user_type_index = type_info->type_index_;
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3938078Z     772. 			int type_size_bits = static_cast<int>(type_info->type_size_);
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3938414Z     773. 
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3938680Z     774. 			// Determine the correct Type value from the found TypeInfo
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3939034Z     775. 			if (type_info->isStruct()) {
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3939403Z     776. 				const StructTypeInfo* struct_info = type_info->getStructInfo();
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3939774Z     777. 				if (struct_info) {
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3940102Z     778. 					type_size_bits = static_cast<int>(struct_info->total_size * 8);
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3940450Z     779. 				}
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3940750Z     780. 				return ParseResult::success(emplace_node<TypeSpecifierNode>(
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3941261Z     781. 					Type::Struct, user_type_index, type_size_bits, type_name_token, cv_qualifier));
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3941823Z     782. 			} else {
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3942103Z     783. 				// Use the type's own Type value (Enum, UserDefined, etc.)
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3942529Z     784. 				return ParseResult::success(emplace_node<TypeSpecifierNode>(
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3943040Z     785. 					type_info->type_, user_type_index, type_size_bits, type_name_token, cv_qualifier));
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3943451Z     786. 			}
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3943620Z     787. 		}
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3943786Z     788. 
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3944050Z     789. 		// Not found - create a placeholder type (forward declaration)
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3944654Z     790. 		TypeInfo& forward_decl_type = add_struct_type(type_name_handle, gSymbolTable.get_current_namespace_handle());
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3945480Z     791. 		return ParseResult::success(emplace_node<TypeSpecifierNode>(
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3945980Z     792. 			Type::Struct, forward_decl_type.type_index_, 0, type_name_token, cv_qualifier));
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3946381Z     793. 	}
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3946711Z     794. 	else if (peek().is_identifier()) {
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3947111Z     795. 		// Handle user-defined type (struct, class, or other user-defined types)
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3947575Z     796. 		// Build qualified name using StringBuilder for efficiency
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3947927Z     797. 		StringBuilder type_name_builder;
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3948246Z     798. 		type_name_builder.append(peek_info().value());
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3948795Z     799. 		Token type_name_token = peek_info();  // Save the token before consuming it
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3949263Z     800. 		Token last_qualified_token = type_name_token;
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3949553Z     801. 		advance();
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3949743Z     802. 
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3950020Z     803. 		// Check for qualified name (e.g., Outer::Inner for nested classes)
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3950520Z     804. 		// Also track if ::template was used (indicates dependent member template access)
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3950969Z     805. 		bool has_explicit_template_keyword = false;
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3951276Z     806. 		while (peek() == "::"_tok) {
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3951531Z     807. 			advance();  // consume '::'
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3951769Z     808. 
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3952004Z     809. 			// Handle ::template for dependent member templates
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3952368Z     810. 			// Example: typename _Tp::template rebind<_Up>
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3952771Z     811. 			// The 'template' keyword is a disambiguator for dependent contexts
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3953149Z     812. 			if (peek() == "template"_tok) {
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3953426Z     813. 				advance();  // consume 'template'
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3953833Z     814. 				has_explicit_template_keyword = true;  // Remember that ::template was used
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3954221Z     815. 			}
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3954389Z     816. 			
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3954580Z     817. 			if (!peek().is_identifier()) {
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3954900Z     818. 				type_name_builder.reset();  // Discard the builder
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3955687Z     819. 				return ParseResult::error("Expected identifier after '::'", peek().is_eof() ? Token() : peek_info());
2026-03-18T16:19:38.3956177Z     820. 			}
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8143825Z 
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8146386Z function:
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8148073Z   name: view
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8149604Z   args:
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8152097Z     path: /home/runner/work/FlashCpp/FlashCpp/src/Parser_TypeSpecifiers.cpp
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8152768Z     view_range: |
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8157168Z       [
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8157454Z         820,
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8159561Z         920
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8160482Z       ]
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8161106Z   result: |
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8161392Z     820. 			}
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8161925Z     821. 
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8162442Z     822. 			type_name_builder.append("::"sv).append(peek_info().value());
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8163485Z     823. 			last_qualified_token = peek_info();
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8163978Z     824. 			advance();
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8164309Z     825. 		}
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8164932Z     826. 
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8165561Z     827. 		// Commit the StringBuilder to get a persistent string_view
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8166433Z     828. 		std::string_view type_name = type_name_builder.commit();
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8166979Z     829. 
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8167582Z     830. 		// Preserve simple dependent qualified member types like T::value_type or T::size_type
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8168558Z     831. 		// as placeholder types so template instantiation can resolve them later.
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8169415Z     832. 		// Without this, they can collapse to bare T before instantiation.
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8170228Z     833. 		if (type_name.find("::") != std::string_view::npos && peek() != "<"_tok &&
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8171101Z     834. 		    (parsing_template_depth_ > 0 || !current_template_param_names_.empty())) {
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8172024Z     835. 			std::string_view base_part = type_name.substr(0, type_name.find("::"));
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8172975Z     836. 			bool is_dependent_qualified_type = false;
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8173647Z     837. 			for (const auto& param_name : current_template_param_names_) {
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8174449Z     838. 				if (param_name == StringTable::getOrInternStringHandle(base_part)) {
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8175457Z     839. 					is_dependent_qualified_type = true;
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8175949Z     840. 					break;
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8176278Z     841. 				}
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8176581Z     842. 			}
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8176873Z     843. 
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8177207Z     844. 			if (is_dependent_qualified_type) {
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8177965Z     845. 				StringHandle type_handle = StringTable::getOrInternStringHandle(type_name);
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8178787Z     846. 				auto type_it = gTypesByName.find(type_handle);
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8179346Z     847. 				TypeIndex type_idx;
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8179819Z     848. 				if (type_it == gTypesByName.end()) {
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8180408Z     849. 					auto& placeholder_type = gTypeInfo.emplace_back();
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8181067Z     850. 					placeholder_type.type_ = Type::UserDefined;
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8181785Z     851. 					placeholder_type.type_index_ = TypeIndex{gTypeInfo.size() - 1};
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8182466Z     852. 					placeholder_type.type_size_ = 0;
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8183002Z     853. 					placeholder_type.name_ = type_handle;
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8183634Z     854. 					placeholder_type.is_incomplete_instantiation_ = true;
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8184325Z     855. 					gTypesByName[type_handle] = &placeholder_type;
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8184932Z     856. 					type_idx = placeholder_type.type_index_;
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8185603Z     857. 				} else {
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8186021Z     858. 					type_idx = type_it->second->type_index_;
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8186500Z     859. 				}
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8186791Z     860. 
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8187267Z     861. 				return ParseResult::success(emplace_node<TypeSpecifierNode>(
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8187911Z     862. 					Type::UserDefined,
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8188330Z     863. 					type_idx,
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8188670Z     864. 					0,
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8189009Z     865. 					last_qualified_token,
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8189441Z     866. 					cv_qualifier));
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8189822Z     867. 			}
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8190122Z     868. 		}
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8190407Z     869. 
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8190797Z     870. 		// Check for template arguments: Container<int>
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8191466Z     871. 		std::optional<std::vector<TemplateTypeArg>> template_args;
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8192065Z     872. 		if (peek() == "<"_tok) {
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8192791Z     873. 			// Before parsing < as template arguments, check if the type name is actually a template
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8193919Z     874. 			// This prevents misinterpreting patterns like _R1::num < _R2::num> where < is comparison
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8194781Z     875. 			bool should_parse_as_template = true;
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8195412Z     876. 			
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8195890Z     877. 			// If ::template was used, always parse < as template arguments
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8196910Z     878. 			// This is the explicit template disambiguator for dependent contexts
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8197612Z     879. 			if (has_explicit_template_keyword) {
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8198140Z     880. 				should_parse_as_template = true;
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8198597Z     881. 			} else {
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8199110Z     882. 				// Check if this is a qualified name (contains ::)
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8199754Z     883. 				size_t last_colon_pos = type_name.rfind("::");
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8200381Z     884. 				if (last_colon_pos != std::string_view::npos) {
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8201016Z     885. 					// Extract the member name (part after the last ::)
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8201768Z     886. 					std::string_view member_name = type_name.substr(last_colon_pos + 2);
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8202402Z     887. 					
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8202789Z     888. 					// Check if the member is a known template
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8203727Z     889. 					auto member_template_opt = gTemplateRegistry.lookupTemplate(member_name);
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8204831Z     890. 					auto member_var_template_opt = gTemplateRegistry.lookupVariableTemplate(member_name);
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8205860Z     891. 					
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8206260Z     892. 					// Also check with the full qualified name
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8206992Z     893. 					auto full_template_opt = gTemplateRegistry.lookupTemplate(type_name);
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8208001Z     894. 					auto full_var_template_opt = gTemplateRegistry.lookupVariableTemplate(type_name);
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8208768Z     895. 					
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8209252Z     896. 					bool member_is_template = member_template_opt.has_value() || 
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8209985Z     897. 					                          member_var_template_opt.has_value() ||
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8210644Z     898. 					                          full_template_opt.has_value() ||
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8211277Z     899. 					                          full_var_template_opt.has_value();
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8211820Z     900. 					
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8212148Z     901. 					if (!member_is_template) {
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8212628Z     902. 						// Member is NOT a known template
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8213383Z     903. 						// Check if the base (before ::) is a template parameter - if so, this is dependent
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8214204Z     904. 						// and we should NOT parse < as template arguments
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8214936Z     905. 						std::string_view base_name = type_name.substr(0, last_colon_pos);
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8215701Z     906. 						
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8216087Z     907. 						// Check if base is a template parameter
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8216637Z     908. 						bool base_is_template_param = false;
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8217285Z     909. 						for (const auto& param_name : current_template_param_names_) {
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8218066Z     910. 							if (StringTable::getStringView(param_name) == base_name) {
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8218713Z     911. 								base_is_template_param = true;
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8219200Z     912. 								break;
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8219542Z     913. 							}
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8219863Z     914. 						}
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8220163Z     915. 						
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8220509Z     916. 						if (base_is_template_param) {
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8221111Z     917. 							// Pattern like _R1::num where _R1 is a template parameter
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8221877Z     918. 							// The member 'num' is likely a static data member, not a template
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8222565Z     919. 							// Treat < as comparison operator
2026-03-18T16:19:42.8223101Z     920. 							FLASH_LOG_FORMAT(Templates, Debug,
2026-03-18T16:19:51.2084292Z 
2026-03-18T16:19:51.2085783Z function:
2026-03-18T16:19:51.2087422Z   name: view
2026-03-18T16:19:51.2089001Z   args:
2026-03-18T16:19:51.2091689Z     path: /home/runner/work/FlashCpp/FlashCpp/src/Parser_TypeSpecifiers.cpp
2026-03-18T16:19:51.2094163Z     view_range: |
2026-03-18T16:19:51.2094482Z       [
2026-03-18T16:19:51.2095397Z         1080,
2026-03-18T16:19:51.2096468Z         1160
2026-03-18T16:19:51.2096785Z       ]
2026-03-18T16:19:51.2097398Z   result: |
2026-03-18T16:19:51.2098303Z     1080. 							// If we went through alias chaining, use the struct name from the instantiated class
2026-03-18T16:19:51.2099329Z     1081. 							std::string_view instantiated_name;
2026-03-18T16:19:51.2099969Z     1082. 							if (instantiated_class->is<StructDeclarationNode>()) {
2026-03-18T16:19:51.2100877Z     1083. 								StringHandle name_handle = instantiated_class->as<StructDeclarationNode>().name();
2026-03-18T16:19:51.2101855Z     1084. 								instantiated_name = StringTable::getStringView(name_handle);
2026-03-18T16:19:51.2102484Z     1085. 							} else {
2026-03-18T16:19:51.2103647Z     1086. 								instantiated_name = get_instantiated_class_name(alias_node.target_template_name(), substituted_args);
2026-03-18T16:19:51.2104539Z     1087. 							}
2026-03-18T16:19:51.2105597Z     1088. 							
2026-03-18T16:19:51.2106459Z     1089. 							// Find the type by scanning gTypeInfo (safer than using gTypesByName pointer)
2026-03-18T16:19:51.2107240Z     1090. 							TypeIndex type_idx {};
2026-03-18T16:19:51.2107689Z     1091. 							bool found = false;
2026-03-18T16:19:51.2108470Z     1092. 							StringHandle target_handle = StringTable::getOrInternStringHandle(instantiated_name);
2026-03-18T16:19:51.2109263Z     1093. 							
2026-03-18T16:19:51.2109689Z     1094. 							for (size_t i = 0; i < gTypeInfo.size(); ++i) {
2026-03-18T16:19:51.2110314Z     1095. 								if (gTypeInfo[i].name() == target_handle) {
2026-03-18T16:19:51.2110852Z     1096. 									type_idx = TypeIndex{i};
2026-03-18T16:19:51.2111292Z     1097. 									found = true;
2026-03-18T16:19:51.2111678Z     1098. 									break;
2026-03-18T16:19:51.2112032Z     1099. 								}
2026-03-18T16:19:51.2112348Z     1100. 							}
2026-03-18T16:19:51.2112647Z     1101. 							
2026-03-18T16:19:51.2112963Z     1102. 							if (found) {
2026-03-18T16:19:51.2113488Z     1103. 								const TypeInfo& new_ti = gTypeInfo[type_idx.value];
2026-03-18T16:19:51.2114064Z     1104. 								
2026-03-18T16:19:51.2114880Z     1105. 								FLASH_LOG(Parser, Debug, "Deferred instantiation succeeded: '", instantiated_name, "' at index ", type_idx);
2026-03-18T16:19:51.2116058Z     1106. 								
2026-03-18T16:19:51.2116555Z     1107. 								// Check for member type access after alias template resolution
2026-03-18T16:19:51.2117294Z     1108. 								// Pattern: typename conditional_t<...>::type
2026-03-18T16:19:51.2117876Z     1109. 								if (peek() == "::"_tok) {
2026-03-18T16:19:51.2118352Z     1110. 									advance(); // consume '::'
2026-03-18T16:19:51.2118785Z     1111. 									
2026-03-18T16:19:51.2119173Z     1112. 									Token member_token = peek_info();
2026-03-18T16:19:51.2119786Z     1113. 									if (member_token.type() == Token::Type::Identifier) {
2026-03-18T16:19:51.2120490Z     1114. 										std::string_view member_name = member_token.value();
2026-03-18T16:19:51.2121121Z     1115. 										advance(); // consume member name
2026-03-18T16:19:51.2121603Z     1116. 										
2026-03-18T16:19:51.2121986Z     1117. 										// Build qualified type name
2026-03-18T16:19:51.2122552Z     1118. 										StringBuilder qualified_name_builder;
2026-03-18T16:19:51.2123240Z     1119. 										std::string_view qualified_type_name = qualified_name_builder
2026-03-18T16:19:51.2123924Z     1120. 											.append(instantiated_name)
2026-03-18T16:19:51.2124421Z     1121. 											.append("::")
2026-03-18T16:19:51.2124840Z     1122. 											.append(member_name)
2026-03-18T16:19:51.2125448Z     1123. 											.commit();
2026-03-18T16:19:51.2125824Z     1124. 										
2026-03-18T16:19:51.2126615Z     1125. 										FLASH_LOG(Parser, Debug, "Looking up member type '", qualified_type_name, "' after alias resolution");
2026-03-18T16:19:51.2127476Z     1126. 										
2026-03-18T16:19:51.2127860Z     1127. 										// Look up the member type
2026-03-18T16:19:51.2128797Z     1128. 										auto member_type_it = gTypesByName.find(StringTable::getOrInternStringHandle(qualified_type_name));
2026-03-18T16:19:51.2130277Z     1129. 										if (member_type_it != gTypesByName.end()) {
2026-03-18T16:19:51.2130984Z     1130. 											const TypeInfo* member_type_info = member_type_it->second;
2026-03-18T16:19:51.2132111Z     1131. 											FLASH_LOG(Parser, Debug, "Found member type '", qualified_type_name, "' at index ", member_type_info->type_index_);
2026-03-18T16:19:51.2133267Z     1132. 											return ParseResult::success(emplace_node<TypeSpecifierNode>(
2026-03-18T16:19:51.2134075Z     1133. 												member_type_info->type_, member_type_info->type_index_, 
2026-03-18T16:19:51.2134856Z     1134. 												static_cast<unsigned char>(member_type_info->type_size_), 
2026-03-18T16:19:51.2135735Z     1135. 												member_token, cv_qualifier));
2026-03-18T16:19:51.2136229Z     1136. 										} else {
2026-03-18T16:19:51.2136764Z     1137. 											// Member type not found - might be a dependent type
2026-03-18T16:19:51.2137992Z     1138. 											FLASH_LOG(Parser, Debug, "Member type '", qualified_type_name, "' not found, creating placeholder");
2026-03-18T16:19:51.2139020Z     1139. 											auto& placeholder_type = gTypeInfo.emplace_back();
2026-03-18T16:19:51.2139706Z     1140. 											placeholder_type.type_ = Type::UserDefined;
2026-03-18T16:19:51.2140477Z     1141. 											placeholder_type.type_index_ = TypeIndex{gTypeInfo.size() - 1};
2026-03-18T16:19:51.2141227Z     1142. 											placeholder_type.type_size_ = 0;
2026-03-18T16:19:51.2142069Z     1143. 											placeholder_type.name_ = StringTable::getOrInternStringHandle(qualified_type_name);
2026-03-18T16:19:51.2143037Z     1144. 											placeholder_type.is_incomplete_instantiation_ = true;
2026-03-18T16:19:51.2143835Z     1145. 											gTypesByName[placeholder_type.name_] = &placeholder_type;
2026-03-18T16:19:51.2144680Z     1146. 											return ParseResult::success(emplace_node<TypeSpecifierNode>(
2026-03-18T16:19:51.2145874Z     1147. 												Type::UserDefined, placeholder_type.type_index_, 0, member_token, cv_qualifier));
2026-03-18T16:19:51.2146649Z     1148. 										}
2026-03-18T16:19:51.2146982Z     1149. 									}
2026-03-18T16:19:51.2147314Z     1150. 								}
2026-03-18T16:19:51.2147631Z     1151. 								
2026-03-18T16:19:51.2148022Z     1152. 								// Create the final type specifier
2026-03-18T16:19:51.2148672Z     1153. 								auto new_type_spec = emplace_node<TypeSpecifierNode>(
2026-03-18T16:19:51.2149283Z     1154. 									Type::Struct,
2026-03-18T16:19:51.2149690Z     1155. 									type_idx,
2026-03-18T16:19:51.2150177Z     1156. 									static_cast<unsigned char>(new_ti.type_size_),
2026-03-18T16:19:51.2150722Z     1157. 									Token(),
2026-03-18T16:19:51.2151101Z     1158. 									CVQualifier::None
2026-03-18T16:19:51.2151520Z     1159. 								);

2026-03-18T16:19:56.9124223Z     1251.

2026-03-18T16:19:56.9124964Z     1253. 						if (member_token.type() == Token::Type::Identifier) {

2026-03-18T16:19:56.9130113Z     1266. 								.append(base_type_name)
2026-03-18T16:19:56.9130376Z     1267. 								.append("::")

2026-03-18T16:19:56.9130874Z     1269. 								.commit();
2026-03-18T16:19:56.9131088Z     1270. 							
2026-03-18T16:19:56.9131597Z     1271. 							FLASH_LOG(Parser, Debug, "Looking up member type '", qualified_type_name, "' after non-deferred alias resolution");
2026-03-18T16:19:56.9132144Z     1272. 							
2026-03-18T16:19:56.9132345Z     1273. 							// Look up the member type
2026-03-18T16:19:56.9132882Z     1274. 							auto member_type_it = gTypesByName.find(StringTable::getOrInternStringHandle(qualified_type_name));
2026-03-18T16:19:56.9133459Z     1275. 							if (member_type_it != gTypesByName.end()) {

2026-03-18T16:19:56.9143046Z     1294. 							}

2026-03-18T16:19:56.9143740Z     1297.

2026-03-18T16:19:56.9144560Z     1299. 				}
2026-03-18T16:19:56.9144733Z     1300. 				
2026-03-18T16:19:56.9145257Z     1301. 				// Check if this is a template parameter being used with template arguments (e.g., Container<T>)
2026-03-18T16:19:56.9145994Z     1302. 				// When parsing a template body, if the type name is a template parameter (type or template template param),
2026-03-18T16:19:56.9146750Z     1303. 				// we should NOT try to instantiate it - it's a dependent type that will be resolved during instantiation
2026-03-18T16:19:56.9147294Z     1304. 				bool is_dependent_template_param = false;
2026-03-18T16:19:56.9147742Z     1305. 				if (parsing_template_depth_ > 0 && !current_template_param_names_.empty()) {
2026-03-18T16:19:56.9148261Z     1306. 					for (const auto& param_name : current_template_param_names_) {
2026-03-18T16:19:56.9148661Z     1307. 						if (param_name == type_name) {
2026-03-18T16:19:56.9148973Z     1308. 							is_dependent_template_param = true;
2026-03-18T16:19:56.9149262Z     1309. 							break;
2026-03-18T16:19:56.9149463Z     1310. 						}
2026-03-18T16:19:56.9149640Z     1311. 					}
2026-03-18T16:19:56.9149818Z     1312. 				}
2026-03-18T16:19:56.9149989Z     1313. 				
2026-03-18T16:19:56.9150192Z     1314. 				if (is_dependent_template_param) {
2026-03-18T16:19:56.9150661Z     1315. 					// This is a template parameter being used with template arguments (e.g., Op<Args...>)
2026-03-18T16:19:56.9151276Z     1316. 					// Check for nested type access (e.g., Op<Args...>::type) before returning early
2026-03-18T16:19:56.9151718Z     1317. 					if (peek() == "::"_tok) {
2026-03-18T16:19:56.9152032Z     1318. 						// Parse the nested type/member access
2026-03-18T16:19:56.9152341Z     1319. 						advance(); // consume '::'
2026-03-18T16:19:56.9152592Z     1320. 						
2026-03-18T16:19:56.9152886Z     1321. 						// Handle optional 'template' keyword for dependent contexts
2026-03-18T16:19:56.9153271Z     1322. 						if (peek() == "template"_tok) {
2026-03-18T16:19:56.9153570Z     1323. 							advance(); // consume 'template'
2026-03-18T16:19:56.9153844Z     1324. 						}
2026-03-18T16:19:56.9154028Z     1325. 						
2026-03-18T16:19:56.9154227Z     1326. 						// Get the nested identifier
2026-03-18T16:19:56.9154510Z     1327. 						if (!peek().is_identifier()) {
2026-03-18T16:19:56.9154936Z     1328. 							return ParseResult::error("Expected identifier after '::'", peek_info());
2026-03-18T16:19:56.9155608Z     1329. 						}
2026-03-18T16:19:56.9155820Z     1330. 						Token nested_token = peek_info();
2026-03-18T16:19:56.9156162Z     1331. 						advance(); // consume the identifier
2026-03-18T16:19:56.9156438Z     1332. 						
2026-03-18T16:19:56.9156693Z     1333. 						// Build a dependent type name: Op<Args...>::type
2026-03-18T16:19:56.9157259Z     1334. 						// For dependent types, we create a placeholder type that will be resolved during instantiation
2026-03-18T16:19:56.9157922Z     1335. 						StringBuilder dependent_type_builder;
2026-03-18T16:19:56.9158267Z     1336. 						dependent_type_builder.append(type_name);
2026-03-18T16:19:56.9158617Z     1337. 						dependent_type_builder.append("<...>::");
2026-03-18T16:19:56.9158999Z     1338. 						dependent_type_builder.append(nested_token.value());
2026-03-18T16:19:56.9159474Z     1339. 						std::string_view dependent_type_name = dependent_type_builder.commit();
2026-03-18T16:19:56.9159868Z     1340.
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3744743Z 
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3746027Z function:
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3747686Z   name: view
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3748231Z   args:
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3748794Z     path: /home/runner/work/FlashCpp/FlashCpp/src/Parser_TypeSpecifiers.cpp
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3749841Z     view_range: |
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3750166Z       [
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3750446Z         1595,
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3750911Z         1700
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3751249Z       ]
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3755366Z   result: |
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3756158Z     1595. 					auto type_it = gTypesByName.find(StringTable::getOrInternStringHandle(instantiated_name));
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3757075Z     1596. 					if (type_it != gTypesByName.end()) {
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3757677Z     1597. 						const TypeInfo* type_info = type_it->second;
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3758470Z     1598. 						// Check if this instantiation has template arguments and any are dependent
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3759281Z     1599. 						if (type_info->isTemplateInstantiation()) {
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3759991Z     1600. 							const auto& template_arg_infos = type_info->templateArgs();
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3760726Z     1601. 							for (const auto& arg_info : template_arg_infos) {
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3761518Z     1602. 								// Check if argument is a UserDefined type (dependent placeholder)
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3762167Z     1603. 								if (arg_info.base_type == Type::UserDefined && arg_info.type_index.value < gTypeInfo.size()) {
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3762703Z     1604. 									has_dependent_args = true;
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3763282Z     1605. 									FLASH_LOG_FORMAT(Templates, Debug, "Instantiated name '{}' has dependent template arguments", instantiated_name);
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3763848Z     1606. 									break;
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3764064Z     1607. 								}
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3764272Z     1608. 							}
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3764456Z     1609. 						}
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3764646Z     1610. 					}
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3764827Z     1611. 				}
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3765467Z     1612. 				// If we're in a template body and the template wasn't found/instantiated,
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3765998Z     1613. 				// treat the type as dependent (e.g., self-referential templates)
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3766627Z     1614. 				if (!has_dependent_args && (parsing_template_depth_ > 0 || !current_template_param_names_.empty())) {
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3767390Z     1615. 					if (!instantiated_class.has_value() && !gTemplateRegistry.lookupTemplate(type_name).has_value()) {
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3767919Z     1616. 						has_dependent_args = true;
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3768497Z     1617. 						FLASH_LOG_FORMAT(Templates, Debug, "Template '{}' not found in template body - treating as dependent", type_name);
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3769286Z     1618. 					}
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3769575Z     1619. 				}
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3769854Z     1620. 				
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3770533Z     1621. 				// Check for qualified name after template arguments: Template<T>::nested or Template<T>::type
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3771366Z     1622. 				if (peek() == "::"_tok) {
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3771973Z     1623. 					// Parse the qualified identifier path (e.g., Template<int>::Inner)
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3772646Z     1624. 					bool had_template_keyword = false;
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3773543Z     1625. 					auto qualified_result = parse_qualified_identifier_after_template(type_name_token, &had_template_keyword);
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3774457Z     1626. 					if (qualified_result.is_error()) {
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3775846Z     1627. 						FLASH_LOG(Parser, Error, "parse_qualified_identifier_after_template failed");
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3776527Z     1628. 						return qualified_result;
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3776790Z     1629. 					}
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3776974Z     1630. 					
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3777284Z     1631. 					// Build fully qualified type name using instantiated template name
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3777841Z     1632. 					const auto& qualified_node = asQualifiedIdentifier(*qualified_result.node());
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3778373Z     1633. 					// Get the qualified namespace name and append the identifier
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3779020Z     1634. 					std::string_view ns_qualified = gNamespaceRegistry.getQualifiedName(qualified_node.namespace_handle());
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3779629Z     1635. 					StringBuilder qualified_type_name_builder;
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3780026Z     1636. 					qualified_type_name_builder.append(instantiated_name);
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3780698Z     1637. 					// If there are additional namespace parts beyond the template, append them
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3781271Z     1638. 					// The namespace handle might include parts beyond just the template name
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3781766Z     1639. 					if (!ns_qualified.empty() && ns_qualified != type_name) {
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3782251Z     1640. 						// Check if ns_qualified starts with type_name:: - if so, append the rest
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3782848Z     1641. 						if (ns_qualified.starts_with(type_name) && ns_qualified.size() > type_name.size() + 2 &&
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3783395Z     1642. 						    ns_qualified.substr(type_name.size(), 2) == "::") {
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3784244Z     1643. 							qualified_type_name_builder.append(ns_qualified.substr(type_name.size()));
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3784680Z     1644. 						}
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3784877Z     1645. 					}
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3785557Z     1646. 					qualified_type_name_builder.append("::").append(qualified_node.identifier_token().value());
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3786199Z     1647. 					std::string_view qualified_type_name = qualified_type_name_builder.commit();
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3786626Z     1648. 					
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3787032Z     1649. 					// For dependent templates, if the qualified type is not found, check for template arguments
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3787536Z     1650. 					// before creating a placeholder
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3787970Z     1651. 					std::string_view member_name = qualified_node.identifier_token().value();
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3788447Z     1652. 					bool has_template_args = (peek() == "<"_tok);
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3788751Z     1653. 					
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3788953Z     1654. 					if (has_dependent_args) {
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3789338Z     1655. 						// Phase 4: Check for lazy nested type instantiation before lookup
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3789879Z     1656. 						// If this is a nested type (e.g., outer_int::inner), try lazy instantiation
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3790522Z     1657. 						StringHandle parent_name_handle = StringTable::getOrInternStringHandle(instantiated_name);
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3791229Z     1658. 						StringHandle nested_name_handle = StringTable::getOrInternStringHandle(member_name);
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3791982Z     1659. 						if (LazyNestedTypeRegistry::getInstance().needsInstantiation(parent_name_handle, nested_name_handle)) {
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3792750Z     1660. 							auto inst_result = instantiateLazyNestedType(parent_name_handle, nested_name_handle);
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3793249Z     1661. 							if (inst_result.has_value()) {
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3793764Z     1662. 								FLASH_LOG(Templates, Debug, "Used lazy nested type instantiation for: ", qualified_type_name);
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3794246Z     1663. 							}
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3794432Z     1664. 						}
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3794616Z     1665. 						
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3794914Z     1666. 						// Phase 3: Check for lazy type alias evaluation before lookup
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3795612Z     1667. 						// If this is a member type alias (e.g., remove_const_int::type), try lazy evaluation
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3796445Z     1668. 						StringHandle class_name_handle = StringTable::getOrInternStringHandle(instantiated_name);
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3797151Z     1669. 						StringHandle member_name_handle_alias = StringTable::getOrInternStringHandle(member_name);
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3797921Z     1670. 						if (LazyTypeAliasRegistry::getInstance().needsEvaluation(class_name_handle, member_name_handle_alias)) {
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3798674Z     1671. 							auto eval_result = evaluateLazyTypeAlias(class_name_handle, member_name_handle_alias);
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3799173Z     1672. 							if (eval_result.has_value()) {
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3799665Z     1673. 								FLASH_LOG(Templates, Debug, "Used lazy type alias evaluation for: ", qualified_type_name);
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3800146Z     1674. 							}
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3800332Z     1675. 						}
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3800512Z     1676. 						
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3800981Z     1677. 						auto qual_type_it = gTypesByName.find(StringTable::getOrInternStringHandle(qualified_type_name));
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3801677Z     1678. 						if (qual_type_it == gTypesByName.end()) {
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3802002Z     1679. 							// Type not found
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3802446Z     1680. 							// If there are template arguments, we need to parse them and include in the type name
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3803007Z     1681. 							// BUT: Check if the member is actually a known template first.
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3803567Z     1682. 							// This avoids misinterpreting comparison operators like `R1::num < R2::num>`
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3804043Z     1683. 							// as template arguments for `num`.
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3804518Z     1684. 							// EXCEPTION: If the 'template' keyword was present (e.g., ::template type<Args>),
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3805253Z     1685. 							// then we MUST treat the member as a template regardless of registry lookup.
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3805743Z     1686. 							if (has_template_args && !had_template_keyword) {
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3806249Z     1687. 								// Check if the member is a known template before parsing < as template arguments
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3806857Z     1688. 								auto member_template_opt = gTemplateRegistry.lookupTemplate(member_name);
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3807508Z     1689. 								auto member_var_template_opt = gTemplateRegistry.lookupVariableTemplate(member_name);
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3807989Z     1690.

2026-03-18T16:20:03.3809426Z     1693. 								auto full_var_template_opt = gTemplateRegistry.lookupVariableTemplate(qualified_type_name);
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3809924Z     1694. 								
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3810229Z     1695. 								bool member_is_template = member_template_opt.has_value() || 
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3810679Z     1696. 								                          member_var_template_opt.has_value() ||
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3811101Z     1697. 								                          full_template_opt.has_value() ||
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3811497Z     1698. 								                          full_var_template_opt.has_value();
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3811823Z     1699. 								
2026-03-18T16:20:03.3812039Z     1700. 								if (!member_is_template) {
2026-03-18T16:20:13.4274080Z 
2026-03-18T16:20:13.4275461Z copilot: OK, now I need to find the exact code path for handling `ns::A::B` as a type specifier for `ns::A::B c;`. The identifier path at line 794 handles this:
2026-03-18T16:20:13.4276432Z 
2026-03-18T16:20:13.4276761Z 1. Builds type_name = "ns::A::B" by concatenating identifiers with "::"
2026-03-18T16:20:13.4294437Z 2. At some point tries to look it up
2026-03-18T16:20:13.4294773Z

2026-03-18T16:20:17.8157229Z     1873. 						if (!member_alias_opt.has_value()) {
2026-03-18T16:20:17.8158378Z     1874. 							auto pattern_name_opt = gTemplateRegistry.get_instantiation_pattern(StringTable::getOrInternStringHandle(instantiated_name));
2026-03-18T16:20:17.8159524Z     1875. 							if (pattern_name_opt.has_value()) {
2026-03-18T16:20:17.8160150Z     1876. 								// This instantiation came from a partial specialization
2026-03-18T16:20:17.8161009Z     1877. 								// Look up the member alias from the pattern
2026-03-18T16:20:17.8161604Z     1878. 								StringBuilder pattern_builder;
2026-03-18T16:20:17.8162719Z     1879. 								std::string_view pattern_member_alias_name = pattern_builder.append(*pattern_name_opt).append("::").append(member_name).preview();
2026-03-18T16:20:17.8164150Z     1880. 								member_alias_opt = gTemplateRegistry.lookup_alias_template(pattern_member_alias_name);
2026-03-18T16:20:17.8164994Z     1881. 								pattern_builder.reset();
2026-03-18T16:20:17.8165751Z     1882. 							}
2026-03-18T16:20:17.8166068Z     1883. 						}
2026-03-18T16:20:17.8166365Z     1884. 						
2026-03-18T16:20:17.8166948Z     1885. 						// If still not found, try with the base template name (for non-partial-spec cases)
2026-03-18T16:20:17.8168357Z     1886. 						// Instantiated names have patterns like "ClassName_int" or "ClassName_int_1"
2026-03-18T16:20:17.8169333Z     1887. 						// We need to find the original template name by progressively stripping suffixes
2026-03-18T16:20:17.8170097Z     1888. 						if (!member_alias_opt.has_value()) {
2026-03-18T16:20:17.8170732Z     1889. 							std::string_view base_template_name = instantiated_name;
2026-03-18T16:20:17.8171303Z     1890. 							
2026-03-18T16:20:17.8171887Z     1891. 							// Try progressively stripping '_suffix' patterns until we find a match
2026-03-18T16:20:17.8172786Z     1892. 							while (!member_alias_opt.has_value() && !base_template_name.empty()) {
2026-03-18T16:20:17.8173634Z     1893. 								size_t underscore_pos = base_template_name.find_last_of('_');
2026-03-18T16:20:17.8174361Z     1894. 								if (underscore_pos == std::string_view::npos) {
2026-03-18T16:20:17.8174969Z     1895. 									break;  // No more underscores to strip
2026-03-18T16:20:17.8175954Z     1896. 								}
2026-03-18T16:20:17.8176271Z     1897. 								
2026-03-18T16:20:17.8176786Z     1898. 								base_template_name = base_template_name.substr(0, underscore_pos);
2026-03-18T16:20:17.8177506Z     1899. 								if (base_template_name.empty()) {
2026-03-18T16:20:17.8178004Z     1900. 									break;
2026-03-18T16:20:17.8178355Z     1901. 								}
2026-03-18T16:20:17.8178661Z     1902. 								
2026-03-18T16:20:17.8179019Z     1903. 								StringBuilder base_builder;
2026-03-18T16:20:17.8180073Z     1904. 								std::string_view base_member_alias_name = base_builder.append(base_template_name).append("::").append(member_name).preview();
2026-03-18T16:20:17.8181460Z     1905. 								member_alias_opt = gTemplateRegistry.lookup_alias_template(base_member_alias_name);
2026-03-18T16:20:17.8182257Z     1906. 								base_builder.reset();
2026-03-18T16:20:17.8182672Z     1907. 							}
2026-03-18T16:20:17.8182979Z     1908. 						}
2026-03-18T16:20:17.8183276Z     1909. 						
2026-03-18T16:20:17.8183660Z     1910. 						if (member_alias_opt.has_value()) {
2026-03-18T16:20:17.8184450Z     1911. 							const TemplateAliasNode& alias_node = member_alias_opt->as<TemplateAliasNode>();
2026-03-18T16:20:17.8185374Z     1912. 							
2026-03-18T16:20:17.8185807Z     1913. 							// Parse template arguments for the member alias
2026-03-18T16:20:17.8186563Z     1914. 							auto member_template_args = parse_explicit_template_arguments();

2026-03-18T16:20:17.8189507Z     1917. 							}
2026-03-18T16:20:17.8189825Z     1918. 							
2026-03-18T16:20:17.8190373Z     1919. 							// Instantiate the member template alias with the provided arguments
2026-03-18T16:20:17.8191301Z     1920. 							TypeSpecifierNode instantiated_type = alias_node.target_type_node();

2026-03-18T16:20:17.8193818Z     1923. 
2026-03-18T16:20:17.8194340Z     1924. 							// Perform substitution for template parameters in the target type
2026-03-18T16:20:17.8195443Z     1925. 							for (size_t i = 0; i < member_template_args->size() && i < param_names.size(); ++i) {
2026-03-18T16:20:17.8196301Z     1926. 								const auto& arg = (*member_template_args)[i];
2026-03-18T16:20:17.8196985Z     1927. 								std::string_view param_name = param_names[i].view();
2026-03-18T16:20:17.8197540Z     1928.

2026-03-18T16:20:17.8198707Z     1930. 								bool is_template_param = false;
2026-03-18T16:20:17.8199676Z     1931. 								if (instantiated_type.type() == Type::UserDefined && instantiated_type.type_index().value < gTypeInfo.size()) {
2026-03-18T16:20:17.8201074Z     1932. 									const TypeInfo& ti = gTypeInfo[instantiated_type.type_index().value];
2026-03-18T16:20:17.8201918Z     1933. 									if (StringTable::getStringView(ti.name()) == param_name) {
2026-03-18T16:20:17.8202563Z     1934. 										is_template_param = true;
2026-03-18T16:20:17.8203011Z     1935. 									}
2026-03-18T16:20:17.8203334Z     1936. 								}
2026-03-18T16:20:17.8203651Z     1937. 								
2026-03-18T16:20:17.8204006Z     1938. 								if (is_template_param) {
2026-03-18T16:20:17.8204586Z     1939. 									// The target type is using this template parameter
2026-03-18T16:20:17.8205342Z     1940. 									if (arg.is_value) {
2026-03-18T16:20:17.8206201Z     1941. 										FLASH_LOG(Parser, Error, "Non-type template arguments not supported in member template aliases yet");

2026-03-18T16:20:17.8208843Z     1943. 									}

2026-03-18T16:20:21.6279882Z     2250. 			} else {

2026-03-18T16:21:14.2069663Z 
2026-03-18T16:21:14.2069908Z copilot: Now I can see! At line 2231:```cpp
2026-03-18T16:21:14.2070643Z const TypeInfo* type_info_ctx = lookupTypeInCurrentContext(type_name_handle);
2026-03-18T16:21:14.2071317Z ```
2026-03-18T16:21:14.2071471Z 
2026-03-18T16:21:14.2072343Z Where `type_name_handle` is "ns::A::B" (built from the tokens). Then `lookupTypeInCurrentContext("ns::A::B")` does:1. Walk namespace chain: current namespace is "ns"
2026-03-18T16:21:14.2073794Z    - Try `ns::ns::A::B` → NOT FOUND
2026-03-18T16:21:14.2075738Z    - Try global `ns::A::B` → NOT FOUND (only "A::B" is registered, not "ns::A::B")2. Direct lookup: `gTypesByName.find("ns::A::B")` → NOT FOUND3. Suffix match: looks for something ending in "::ns::A::B" → NOT FOUNDReturns nullptr.
2026-03-18T16:21:14.2078942Z 
2026-03-18T16:21:14.2078951Z 
2026-03-18T16:21:14.2083527Z The parser then fails to find the type and falls through. But if I fix the nested struct registration to store the full qualified name "ns::A::B", then the direct lookup will succeed. Let me trace through the namespace chain logic more carefully though—when building `ns::type_name` where `type_name` is already "ns::A::B", that creates the wrong path.
2026-03-18T16:21:14.2086076Z 
2026-03-18T16:21:14.2087772Z Now I need to handle the typedef and using alias case. I'm building qualified alias names by combining the struct reference name with the alias name, then registering that qualified alias. The issue is that `struct_ref->name()` only returns the simple struct name without the namespace prefix.
2026-03-18T16:21:14.2089800Z 
2026-03-18T16:21:14.2091478Z Since `parse_member_type_alias` is a Parser member function and gets called while the struct is already on the parsing context stack, I can walk through `struct_parsing_context_stack_` to build the full qualified name. I'll also need access to `current_namespace_handle` to get the complete path.
2026-03-18T16:21:14.2093359Z 
2026-03-18T16:21:14.2094119Z Let me outline the fixes I need to make. First, for nested struct type_name building in the relevant section of Parser_Decl_StructEnum.cpp...
2026-03-18T16:21:14.2095268Z 
2026-03-18T16:21:14.2101045Z Building the qualified struct chain... The emplace call gets skipped since we already registered "ns::A::B" earlier, which is the expected behavior. This confirms Fix 1 handles multi-level nesting correctly. Now looking at Fix 3 for typedef and using aliases—there are multiple code paths where `register_type_alias` gets called, and the one at line 406 for `using Alias = Type;` constructs a qualified alias name by appending the struct's simple name...
2026-03-18T16:21:14.2106356Z

2026-03-18T16:21:14.2107375Z

2026-03-18T16:21:14.2109452Z 
2026-03-18T16:21:14.2111133Z Looking at how `struct_ref->name()` works, it returns just the simple struct name like "Container", but when the struct was originally parsed in a namespace, the TypeInfo already has the full qualified name "ns::Container" stored. I need to use that fully qualified struct name as the base instead of building it piecemeal.
2026-03-18T16:21:14.2113037Z

Let me verify this more carefully by testing the actual behavior.

2026-03-18T16:21:14.8227337Z     [DEBUG][Codegen] %3 = call @check_alias(int32 %Ok)
2026-03-18T16:21:14.8227932Z     [ERROR][Codegen] Missing variable name: 'Ok', not in local or global scope
2026-03-18T16:21:14.8228693Z     [ERROR][Codegen] Code generation error in function, skipping: Missing variable: Ok
2026-03-18T16:21:14.8229458Z     [ERROR][Codegen] Missing variable name: 'Ok', not in local or global scope
2026-03-18T16:21:14.8230208Z     [ERROR][Codegen] Code generation error in function, skipping: Missing variable: Ok
2026-03-18T16:21:14.8231534Z     [ERROR][Codegen] Code generation error in function, skipping: handleFunctionCall register arg: variable not found in variables map: Ok
2026-03-18T16:21:14.8232784Z     <exited with exit code 0>
2026-03-18T16:22:05.0517591Z 
2026-03-18T16:22:05.0521256Z copilot: Ah, now I see the issue! It's not a type lookup failure, but rather `generateQualifiedIdentifierIr: struct_or_enum_name='AliasStatus', found=0`. This means when trying to access `Container::AliasStatus::Ok`, the codegen can't find the enumerator `Ok` under the alias name "AliasStatus" (it looks up by the alias name, not the underlying type name "Status").
2026-03-18T16:22:05.0524656Z 
2026-03-18T16:22:05.0527647Z The issue is that the `AliasStatus` TypeInfo is a type alias (created by `register_type_alias`) that wraps the Status enum. When the codegen tries to find the enumerators for `AliasStatus`, it looks at the TypeInfo, which is a Type::Enum with `type_index_` = original Status TypeIndex. But then it needs to look up the EnumTypeInfo to find the enumerators, and the alias TypeInfo might not have a proper EnumTypeInfo.
2026-03-18T16:22:05.0530997Z  
2026-03-18T16:22:05.0533569Z So when the function parameter has type `AliasStatus`, the codegen stores the alias's type_index rather than the original Status type_index. When it later tries to resolve `Container::AliasStatus::Ok`, it searches for enumerators using the alias type_index, but the EnumTypeInfo is only registered under the original Status type_index, so the lookup fails.
2026-03-18T16:22:05.0536138Z 
2026-03-18T16:22:05.0537296Z The real problem is that enumerators are registered under the original enum name "Status", not the alias name "AliasStatus", so when codegen tries to find "Ok" within "AliasStatus", it can't locate it.

2026-03-18T16:22:15.1075828Z     1246. 		if (!ns_handle.isGlobal()) {
2026-03-18T16:22:15.1076295Z     1247. 			// The struct/enum name is the last namespace component (the name of the namespace handle)

2026-03-18T16:22:15.1077322Z     1249. 
2026-03-18T16:22:15.1077548Z     1250. 			// Could be EnumName::EnumeratorName
2026-03-18T16:22:15.1077953Z     1251. 			// Check the codegen-local symbol table first before gTypesByName.
2026-03-18T16:22:15.1078955Z     1252. 			// gTypesByName uses unordered_map::emplace which is a no-op on duplicate
2026-03-18T16:22:15.1079640Z     1253. 			// keys, so when two functions define `enum class Priority`, the second
2026-03-18T16:22:15.1080698Z     1254. 			// function's TypeInfo is never registered under "Priority" — it always
2026-03-18T16:22:15.1081473Z     1255. 			// resolves to the first function's TypeInfo.
2026-03-18T16:22:15.1082285Z     1256. 			// visitEnumDeclarationNode inserts the correct TypeInfo (via TypeIndex
2026-03-18T16:22:15.1083220Z     1257. 			// stored in the AST node at parse time) into the local symbol table,
2026-03-18T16:22:15.1084040Z     1258. 			// so we must prefer that over the global gTypesByName lookup.
2026-03-18T16:22:15.1084738Z     1259. 			TypeInfo* scoped_enum_type_info = nullptr;
2026-03-18T16:22:15.1085461Z     1260. 			{

2026-03-18T16:22:15.1088058Z     1263. 					const auto& decl = local_sym->as<DeclarationNode>();
2026-03-18T16:22:15.1088714Z     1264. 					if (decl.type_node().is<TypeSpecifierNode>()) {
2026-03-18T16:22:15.1089383Z     1265. 						const auto& ts = decl.type_node().as<TypeSpecifierNode>();
2026-03-18T16:22:15.1090271Z     1266. 						if (ts.type() == Type::Enum && ts.type_index().is_valid() && ts.type_index().value < gTypeInfo.size())

2026-03-18T16:22:15.1091750Z     1268. 					}
2026-03-18T16:22:15.1092052Z     1269. 				}

2026-03-18T16:22:15.1099224Z     1284. 				}
2026-03-18T16:22:15.1099408Z     1285. 			}
2026-03-18T16:22:15.1099580Z     1286. 
2026-03-18T16:22:15.1100005Z     1287. 			// Check if this is a static member access (e.g., StructName::static_member or ns::StructName::static_member)

2026-03-18T16:22:15.1123084Z     1339. 							if (it->second->type_index_ < best_type_index) {
2026-03-18T16:22:15.1123444Z     1340. 								best_type_index = it->second->type_index_;
2026-03-18T16:22:45.2379598Z 
2026-03-18T16:22:45.2380844Z copilot: I see! At line 1248, `struct_or_enum_name = gNamespaceRegistry.getName(ns_handle)`. This gets the SHORT name of the namespace - just the last component.
2026-03-18T16:22:45.2381769Z 
2026-03-18T16:22:45.2383017Z The debug message "Found struct with full qualified name: Container::AliasStatus" at the debug output means the code found it via the gNamespaceRegistry depth check (line 1292-1317). It gets the full name "Container::AliasStatus" from the namespace registry.
2026-03-18T16:22:45.2384407Z 
2026-03-18T16:22:45.2384797Z Then it looks up "Container::AliasStatus" in gTypesByName and finds it (it's a type alias). 
2026-03-18T16:22:45.2385666Z 
2026-03-18T16:22:45.2386524Z But then the problem is that it's a STRUCT type... wait, no - the check at line 1273 is for enums: `type_it->second->isEnum()`. 
2026-03-18T16:22:45.2387214Z 
2026-03-18T16:22:45.2394665Z So the issue is that the lookup at line 1271 is searching for just "AliasStatus" in gTypesByName, but the type is actually registered under the full qualified name "Container::AliasStatus". That's why it's not finding the enum alias - it's looking for the wrong key. I'm realizing the issue now—the code at line 1292-1317 only executes under certain conditions, and I need to trace through what's actually happening when it looks up "Container::AliasStatus" versus just "AliasStatus" in the type registry. The debug output showing `found=0` for "AliasStatus" suggests the lookup is using a different key than what got registered during the depth check, which means there's a mismatch in how the qualified name is being constructed or retrieved at different points in the code. I'm realizing these debug messages might actually be from separate code paths—the first one checking if `AliasStatus` is a struct for member access, and the second from a different operation entirely. Let me trace through what happens after `struct_type_it` gets found to understand the actual flow.

2026-03-18T16:22:45.2481398Z     1372. 					auto [static_member, owner_struct] = struct_info->findStaticMemberRecursive(StringTable::getOrInternStringHandle(qualifiedIdNode.name()));
