2026-03-18T16:47:31.3176360Z function:
2026-03-18T16:47:31.3184347Z   name: view
2026-03-18T16:47:31.3185466Z   args:
2026-03-18T16:47:31.3187810Z     path: /home/runner/work/FlashCpp/FlashCpp/docs/MISSING_FEATURES.md
2026-03-18T16:47:31.3190010Z     view_range: |
2026-03-18T16:47:31.3190360Z       [
2026-03-18T16:47:31.3190646Z         415,
2026-03-18T16:47:31.3190941Z         445
2026-03-18T16:47:31.3191226Z       ]
2026-03-18T16:47:31.3191511Z   result: |
2026-03-18T16:47:31.3191817Z     415. ## Known Issues
2026-03-18T16:47:31.3192217Z     416. 
2026-03-18T16:47:31.3192807Z     417. ### ~~Operator ADL does not find regular free-function operators in associated namespaces~~ (FIXED)
2026-03-18T16:47:31.3193286Z     418. 
2026-03-18T16:47:31.3193481Z     419. **Status**: Fixed (2026-03-18)
2026-03-18T16:47:31.3193737Z     420. 
2026-03-18T16:47:31.3195326Z     421. Operator ADL now uses `lookup_adl()` (full ADL including both hidden friends and regular namespace-scoped operators) instead of `lookup_adl_only()` in `findBinaryOperatorOverloadWithFreeFunction`. Results are deduplicated against the `lookup_all()` scope-chain results to avoid false ambiguity. Test: `test_operator_adl_free_function_ret0.cpp`.
2026-03-18T16:47:31.3196760Z     422. 
2026-03-18T16:47:31.3197140Z     423. ### ~~Enum ADL may fail for enums declared inside class bodies or anonymous namespaces~~ (FIXED)
2026-03-18T16:47:31.3197586Z     424. 
2026-03-18T16:47:31.3197763Z     425. **Status**: Fixed (2026-03-18)
2026-03-18T16:47:31.3198007Z     426. 
2026-03-18T16:47:31.3200612Z     427. Enum ADL for enums declared inside class bodies now works correctly. The fix registers nested enums in `gTypesByName` with their struct-qualified name (e.g., `ns::Container::Status`) in addition to the simple name, so that `lookupTypeInCurrentContext` resolves qualified type references like `Container::Status` to the correct enum `TypeInfo` with proper `type_index`. The ADL machinery (`lookup_adl` / `lookup_adl_only`) already correctly uses `TypeInfo::namespaceHandle()` which is set to the innermost enclosing namespace by `get_current_namespace_handle()` (which skips class scopes). Test: `test_hidden_friend_enum_adl_ret0.cpp`.
2026-03-18T16:47:31.3202930Z     428. 
2026-03-18T16:47:31.3203244Z     429. ### ~~Local (function-scoped) enum declarations are not supported~~ (FIXED)
2026-03-18T16:47:31.3203628Z     430. 
2026-03-18T16:47:31.3203805Z     431. **Status**: Fixed (2026-03-17)
2026-03-18T16:47:31.3204045Z     432. 
2026-03-18T16:47:31.3205903Z     433. Local `enum` and `enum class` declarations inside function bodies are now fully supported. Both unscoped and scoped (enum class) variants work, including explicit underlying types and qualified access (`EnumClass::Value`). Enumerator symbols are properly re-inserted into the codegen-local symbol table when the enum declaration is visited during code generation.
2026-03-18T16:47:31.3207340Z     434. 
2026-03-18T16:47:31.3207517Z     435. ---
2026-03-18T16:47:31.3207692Z     436. 
2026-03-18T16:47:31.3207870Z     437. ## References
2026-03-18T16:47:31.3208067Z     438. 
2026-03-18T16:47:31.3208319Z     439. **Parser Code**: `src/Parser.cpp` - Main parsing logic
2026-03-18T16:47:31.3208750Z     440. **Parser Types**: `src/ParserTypes.h` - Shared parsing structures
2026-03-18T16:47:31.3209184Z     441. **AST Definitions**: `src/AstNodeTypes.h` - AST node types
2026-03-18T16:47:31.3209606Z     442. **Code Generator**: `src/IrGenerator.h` - AST to IR translation
2026-03-18T16:47:31.3210067Z     443. **IR Converter**: `src/IRConverter.h` - IR to assembly conversion
2026-03-18T16:47:31.3210600Z     444. **ELF Writer**: `src/ElfFileWriter.h` - ELF object file generation (exception tables, DWARF)
2026-03-18T16:47:31.3211221Z     445. **LSDA Generator**: `src/LSDAGenerator.h` - Language-Specific Data Area for exceptions
2026-03-18T16:47:53.1910867Z 
2026-03-18T16:47:53.1912389Z function:
2026-03-18T16:47:53.1913629Z   name: edit
2026-03-18T16:47:53.1913995Z   args:
2026-03-18T16:47:53.1916292Z     new_str: |
2026-03-18T16:47:53.1916642Z       ## Known Issues
2026-03-18T16:47:53.1917012Z       
2026-03-18T16:47:53.1917795Z       ### ~~Operator ADL does not find regular free-function operators in associated namespaces~~ (FIXED)
2026-03-18T16:47:53.1918672Z       
2026-03-18T16:47:53.1918998Z       **Status**: Fixed (2026-03-18)
2026-03-18T16:47:53.1919431Z       
2026-03-18T16:47:53.1922861Z       Operator ADL now uses `lookup_adl()` (full ADL including both hidden friends and regular namespace-scoped operators) instead of `lookup_adl_only()` in `findBinaryOperatorOverloadWithFreeFunction`. Results are deduplicated against the `lookup_all()` scope-chain results to avoid false ambiguity. Dedup uses mangled name for functions that have been mangled, and stable pointer identity as a fallback for pre-mangling candidates. Test: `test_operator_adl_free_function_ret0.cpp`.
2026-03-18T16:47:53.1926455Z       
2026-03-18T16:47:53.1927074Z       ### ~~Enum ADL may fail for enums declared inside class bodies or anonymous namespaces~~ (FIXED)
2026-03-18T16:47:53.1927820Z       
2026-03-18T16:47:53.1928119Z       **Status**: Fixed (2026-03-18)
2026-03-18T16:47:53.1928528Z       
2026-03-18T16:47:53.1932423Z       Enum ADL for enums declared inside class bodies now works correctly. The fix registers nested enums in `gTypesByName` with their struct-qualified name (e.g., `ns::Container::Status`) in addition to the simple name, so that `lookupTypeInCurrentContext` resolves qualified type references like `Container::Status` to the correct enum `TypeInfo` with proper `type_index`. The ADL machinery (`lookup_adl` / `lookup_adl_only`) already correctly uses `TypeInfo::namespaceHandle()` which is set to the innermost enclosing namespace by `get_current_namespace_handle()` (which skips class scopes). Test: `test_hidden_friend_enum_adl_ret0.cpp`.
2026-03-18T16:47:53.1936967Z       
2026-03-18T16:47:53.1937545Z       ### ~~Deeply nested struct types (3+ levels) in namespaces not resolvable~~ (FIXED)
2026-03-18T16:47:53.1938246Z       
2026-03-18T16:47:53.1938557Z       **Status**: Fixed (2026-03-18)
2026-03-18T16:47:53.1938975Z       
2026-03-18T16:47:53.1942620Z       When a struct was nested more than one level deep (e.g., `struct A { struct B { struct C {}; }; }` in namespace `ns`), the type `C` was registered as `B::C` (using only the immediate parent) instead of `ns::A::B::C`. This caused `ns::A::B::C c;` to fail with "Missing identifier". The fix walks the full `struct_parsing_context_stack_` (which stores simple names for each nesting level) to build the complete struct chain, then prepends the namespace via `gNamespaceRegistry.buildQualifiedIdentifier`. Tests: `test_2level_ns_struct_ret0.cpp`, `test_3level_ns_struct_ret0.cpp`.
2026-03-18T16:47:53.1946382Z       
2026-03-18T16:47:53.1947063Z       ### ~~typedef/using aliases for nested types not registered with namespace-qualified names~~ (FIXED)
2026-03-18T16:47:53.1947890Z       
2026-03-18T16:47:53.1948206Z       **Status**: Fixed (2026-03-18)
2026-03-18T16:47:53.1948689Z       
2026-03-18T16:47:53.1951569Z       `using AliasType = OriginalType` inside a struct was registered in `gTypesByName` only as `StructName::AliasType` (using the simple struct name, not the full chain). This meant code in a different namespace could not resolve `ns::Container::AliasType`. The fix walks `struct_parsing_context_stack_` to build the full struct chain and also registers the namespace-qualified alias name `ns::Container::AliasType`. Test: `test_typedef_alias_adl_ret0.cpp`.
2026-03-18T16:47:53.1954539Z       
2026-03-18T16:47:53.1956693Z       **Note**: Direct enum *value* access through an alias (`Container::AliasStatus::Ok`) is not yet supported for unscoped enums — the underlying enumerators are not tracked through type aliases. Scoped enum aliases work correctly.
2026-03-18T16:47:53.1958317Z       
2026-03-18T16:47:53.1958831Z       ### ~~Local (function-scoped) enum declarations are not supported~~ (FIXED)
2026-03-18T16:47:53.1959504Z     old_str: |
2026-03-18T16:47:53.1959829Z       ## Known Issues
2026-03-18T16:47:53.1960184Z       
2026-03-18T16:47:53.1960841Z       ### ~~Operator ADL does not find regular free-function operators in associated namespaces~~ (FIXED)
2026-03-18T16:47:53.1961648Z       
2026-03-18T16:47:53.1961956Z       **Status**: Fixed (2026-03-18)
2026-03-18T16:47:53.1962375Z       
2026-03-18T16:47:53.1964675Z       Operator ADL now uses `lookup_adl()` (full ADL including both hidden friends and regular namespace-scoped operators) instead of `lookup_adl_only()` in `findBinaryOperatorOverloadWithFreeFunction`. Results are deduplicated against the `lookup_all()` scope-chain results to avoid false ambiguity. Test: `test_operator_adl_free_function_ret0.cpp`.
2026-03-18T16:47:53.1967319Z       
2026-03-18T16:47:53.1967955Z       ### ~~Enum ADL may fail for enums declared inside class bodies or anonymous namespaces~~ (FIXED)
2026-03-18T16:47:53.1968738Z       
2026-03-18T16:47:53.1969042Z       **Status**: Fixed (2026-03-18)
2026-03-18T16:47:53.1969458Z       
2026-03-18T16:47:53.1973499Z       Enum ADL for enums declared inside class bodies now works correctly. The fix registers nested enums in `gTypesByName` with their struct-qualified name (e.g., `ns::Container::Status`) in addition to the simple name, so that `lookupTypeInCurrentContext` resolves qualified type references like `Container::Status` to the correct enum `TypeInfo` with proper `type_index`. The ADL machinery (`lookup_adl` / `lookup_adl_only`) already correctly uses `TypeInfo::namespaceHandle()` which is set to the innermost enclosing namespace by `get_current_namespace_handle()` (which skips class scopes). Test: `test_hidden_friend_enum_adl_ret0.cpp`.
2026-03-18T16:47:53.1977929Z       
2026-03-18T16:47:53.1978453Z       ### ~~Local (function-scoped) enum declarations are not supported~~ (FIXED)
2026-03-18T16:47:53.1979323Z     path: /home/runner/work/FlashCpp/FlashCpp/docs/MISSING_FEATURES.md
2026-03-18T16:47:53.1980329Z   result: File /home/runner/work/FlashCpp/FlashCpp/docs/MISSING_FEATURES.md updated with changes.
2026-03-18T16:48:02.7038857Z

2026-03-18T16:48:03.1163090Z     KNOWN_ISSUES.md				  _TYPE_LOOKUP_OPTIMIZATION_PLAN_UPDATED_done.md
2026-03-18T16:48:03.1163677Z     LEXER_OPTIMIZATION_PROPOSAL.md		  _TYPE_LOOKUP_OPTIMIZATION_PLAN_done.md
2026-03-18T16:48:03.1164180Z     MISSING_FEATURES.md			  known_ir_issues.md
2026-03-18T16:48:03.1164564Z     MULTIPLE_CATCH_HANDLER_STATUS.md	  non_standard

2026-03-18T16:48:03.1165419Z     <exited with exit code 0>
2026-03-18T16:48:08.5017260Z 
2026-03-18T16:48:08.5017780Z function:
2026-03-18T16:48:08.5018175Z   name: bash
2026-03-18T16:48:08.5018522Z   args:
2026-03-18T16:48:08.5019024Z     command: tail -20 /home/runner/work/FlashCpp/FlashCpp/docs/KNOWN_ISSUES.md
2026-03-18T16:48:08.5019873Z     description: Check KNOWN_ISSUES.md for adding alias enum note
2026-03-18T16:48:08.5020437Z   result: |
2026-03-18T16:48:08.5020647Z     ```cpp
2026-03-18T16:48:08.5020871Z     template<typename T>
2026-03-18T16:48:08.5021136Z     void f() {
2026-03-18T16:48:08.5021887Z         T(x)[3];   // C++20: declares x as T[3] — NOT a cast + subscript expression
2026-03-18T16:48:08.5022463Z         T(y);      // C++20: declares y as T    — NOT a functional cast
2026-03-18T16:48:08.5022832Z     }
2026-03-18T16:48:08.5023079Z     ```
2026-03-18T16:48:08.5023273Z     
2026-03-18T16:48:08.5023629Z     Per C++20 [dcl.ambig.res], when a statement is syntactically ambiguous between
2026-03-18T16:48:08.5024230Z     a declaration and an expression, it shall be treated as a declaration.
2026-03-18T16:48:08.5024835Z     `T(x)[3]` should therefore declare `x` as `T[3]`, but `parse_variable_declaration`
2026-03-18T16:48:08.5025756Z     currently expects a plain identifier directly after the type specifier and does
2026-03-18T16:48:08.5026276Z     not recognise the `(identifier)` declarator syntax.
2026-03-18T16:48:08.5026602Z     
2026-03-18T16:48:08.5026946Z     The disambiguation routing in `parse_statement_or_declaration` is **correct**
2026-03-18T16:48:08.5027543Z     (it no longer misidentifies `[` as an expression-only token), and a `_fail` test
2026-03-18T16:48:08.5028148Z     (`test_tparam_bracket_decl_ambig_fail.cpp`) documents the current parse error.
2026-03-18T16:48:08.5028759Z     The remaining work is to extend `parse_variable_declaration` (and the declarator
2026-03-18T16:48:08.5029687Z     parser) to handle parenthesized declarators as defined in [dcl.decl]/[dcl.paren].
2026-03-18T16:48:08.5030372Z     <exited with exit code 0>
2026-03-18T16:48:21.9650799Z 
2026-03-18T16:48:21.9651266Z function:
2026-03-18T16:48:21.9651561Z   name: bash
2026-03-18T16:48:21.9651830Z   args:
2026-03-18T16:48:21.9652838Z     command: |
2026-03-18T16:48:21.9653491Z       cat >> /home/runner/work/FlashCpp/FlashCpp/docs/KNOWN_ISSUES.md << 'EOF'
2026-03-18T16:48:21.9654303Z       
2026-03-18T16:48:21.9654800Z       ## Unscoped enum enumerator access through type aliases
2026-03-18T16:48:21.9655657Z       
2026-03-18T16:48:21.9656249Z       Accessing unscoped enum values using a type alias as the qualifier
2026-03-18T16:48:21.9657292Z       (`Container::AliasStatus::Ok` where `AliasStatus = Status`) does not work.
2026-03-18T16:48:21.9658364Z       The alias `TypeInfo` does not carry an `EnumTypeInfo` (enumerators are only
2026-03-18T16:48:21.9659768Z       tracked on the original enum's `TypeInfo`), so the codegen cannot resolve the
2026-03-18T16:48:21.9660527Z       enumerator value.
2026-03-18T16:48:21.9660790Z       
2026-03-18T16:48:21.9661001Z       ```cpp
2026-03-18T16:48:21.9661238Z       struct Container {
2026-03-18T16:48:21.9661564Z           enum Status { Ok, Fail };          // unscoped
2026-03-18T16:48:21.9661957Z           using AliasStatus = Status;
2026-03-18T16:48:21.9662276Z       };
2026-03-18T16:48:21.9662589Z       // Works:    Container::Status::Ok
2026-03-18T16:48:21.9663237Z       // Broken:   Container::AliasStatus::Ok  (no EnumTypeInfo on alias)
2026-03-18T16:48:21.9663809Z       ```
2026-03-18T16:48:21.9664089Z       
2026-03-18T16:48:21.9664709Z       Scoped enums (`enum class`) work through aliases because the enumerator lookup
2026-03-18T16:48:21.9665818Z       uses a different code path (integer constant from `EnumTypeInfo`).
2026-03-18T16:48:21.9666534Z       
2026-03-18T16:48:21.9667144Z       **Workaround**: use the original enum name (`Container::Status::Ok`) or use
2026-03-18T16:48:21.9667903Z       scoped enums (`enum class`).
2026-03-18T16:48:21.9668312Z       EOF
2026-03-18T16:48:21.9668736Z     description: Document the unscoped enum alias limitation
2026-03-18T16:48:21.9669318Z   result: <exited with exit code 0>
2026-03-18T16:48:29.0406962Z 
2026-03-18T16:48:29.0407718Z copilot: Now let me run the full test suite once more to verify everything still passes:

This generates the correct type_name `ns::A::B::C` for any nesting depth (not just `B::C` which broke ns-qualified lookup). Also fixes 2-level nesting within namespaces.
2026-03-18T16:50:15.2899894Z

2026-03-18T16:50:15.2914470Z     - In src/Parser_Decl_TypedefUsing.cpp, lines 401-403: This comment explains why the simple alias name is not registered, but the code at line 433 appears to do exactly that with `register_type_alias(qualified_alias_name, final_type_spec)` in the non-struct case. Consider clarifying that this restriction only applies to struct-nested aliases or explaining why the non-struct case is safe.
2026-03-18T16:50:15.2919161Z     - In src/Parser_Decl_TypedefUsing.cpp, line 419: The return value of `register_type_alias` is used here but the function's return value is discarded at line 433. 2026-03-18T16:50:20.4115874Z     393. 		// (re-get type_spec since we modified it above)
2026-03-18T16:50:20.4116824Z     394. 		const TypeSpecifierNode& final_type_spec = type_result.node()->as<TypeSpecifierNode>();
2026-03-18T16:50:20.4117617Z     395. 		
2026-03-18T16:50:20.4118050Z     396. 		// Build qualified name if we're inside a struct.
2026-03-18T16:50:20.4118885Z     397. 		// Walk struct_parsing_context_stack_ (which includes the current struct since it
2026-03-18T16:50:20.4119882Z     398. 		// was pushed before member parsing begins) to build the full struct chain at any
2026-03-18T16:50:20.4120762Z     399. 		// depth, then also register a namespace-prefixed version for ADL.
2026-03-18T16:50:20.4121700Z     400. 		// This mirrors the registration done for direct enum declarations (StructEnum.cpp:770-788).
2026-03-18T16:50:20.4122675Z     401. 		// We do NOT register the simple alias name here: that would make unscoped enum
2026-03-18T16:50:20.4123570Z     402. 		// enumerator lookup find the alias TypeInfo (no EnumTypeInfo) instead of the
2026-03-18T16:50:20.4124285Z     403. 		// original enum TypeInfo, causing codegen crashes.
2026-03-18T16:50:20.4124875Z     404. 		StringHandle qualified_alias_name = alias_name;
2026-03-18T16:50:20.4125522Z     405. 		if (struct_ref) {
2026-03-18T16:50:20.4126419Z     406. 			NamespaceHandle current_ns = gSymbolTable.get_current_namespace_handle();
2026-03-18T16:50:20.4127394Z     407. 			std::string_view current_ns_name = gNamespaceRegistry.getQualifiedName(current_ns);
2026-03-18T16:50:20.4128115Z     408. 
2026-03-18T16:50:20.4128667Z     409. 			// Build struct-chain-relative name (e.g., "Container::AliasStatus" for 1 level,
2026-03-18T16:50:20.4129478Z     410. 			// "A::B::AliasStatus" for 2 levels of struct nesting)
2026-03-18T16:50:20.4130017Z     411. 			StringBuilder chain_builder;
2026-03-18T16:50:20.4130561Z     412. 			for (const auto& ctx : struct_parsing_context_stack_) {
2026-03-18T16:50:20.4131211Z     413. 				chain_builder.append(ctx.struct_name).append("::");
2026-03-18T16:50:20.4131732Z     414. 			}
2026-03-18T16:50:20.4132065Z     415. 			chain_builder.append(alias_name);
2026-03-18T16:50:20.4132921Z     416. 			StringHandle struct_relative_handle = StringTable::getOrInternStringHandle(chain_builder.commit());
2026-03-18T16:50:20.4133981Z     417. 
2026-03-18T16:50:20.4134560Z     418. 			// Always register the struct-chain-relative name (replaces old back()-based build)
2026-03-18T16:50:20.4157014Z     419. 			TypeInfo& alias_info = register_type_alias(struct_relative_handle, final_type_spec);
2026-03-18T16:50:20.4157790Z     420. 
2026-03-18T16:50:20.4158115Z     421. 			if (!current_ns_name.empty()) {
2026-03-18T16:50:20.4158860Z     422. 				// Also register namespace-qualified name "ns::Container::AliasStatus" so that
2026-03-18T16:50:20.4159725Z     423. 				// ADL and type lookups work from outside the namespace.
2026-03-18T16:50:20.4160610Z     424. 				StringHandle ns_qualified_handle = gNamespaceRegistry.buildQualifiedIdentifier(
2026-03-18T16:50:20.4161423Z     425. 					current_ns, struct_relative_handle);
2026-03-18T16:50:20.4162108Z     426. 				if (gTypesByName.find(ns_qualified_handle) == gTypesByName.end()) {
2026-03-18T16:50:20.4162912Z     427. 					gTypesByName.emplace(ns_qualified_handle, &alias_info);
2026-03-18T16:50:20.4163483Z     428. 				}
2026-03-18T16:50:20.4163767Z     429. 			}
2026-03-18T16:50:20.4164125Z     430. 			return ParseResult::success();
2026-03-18T16:50:20.4164543Z     431. 		}
2026-03-18T16:50:20.4164832Z     432. 		
2026-03-18T16:50:20.4165417Z     433. 		register_type_alias(qualified_alias_name, final_type_spec);
2026-03-18T16:50:20.4165983Z     434. 		
2026-03-18T16:50:20.4166306Z     435. 		return ParseResult::success();
2026-03-18T16:50:20.4166716Z     436. 	}
2026-03-18T16:50:20.4167002Z     437. 	
2026-03-18T16:50:20.4167447Z     438. 	// For 'typedef', check if this is an inline struct/enum definition
2026-03-18T16:50:20.4168108Z     439. 	// Pattern: typedef struct { ... } Alias;
2026-03-18T16:50:20.4168640Z     440. 	// Pattern: typedef enum { ... } Alias;
2026-03-18T16:50:20.4169105Z     441. 	if (!peek().is_eof() && 
2026-03-18T16:50:20.4169717Z     442. 	    (peek() == "struct"_tok || peek() == "class"_tok || peek() == "enum"_tok)) {
2026-03-18T16:50:20.4170704Z     443. 		// This is potentially an inline definition - use the full parse_typedef_declaration logic
2026-03-18T16:50:20.4171617Z     444. 		// We already consumed 'typedef', so we need to restore it
2026-03-18T16:50:20.4172354Z     445. 		// Actually, we can't restore easily, so let's handle it inline here
2026-03-18T16:50:20.4172964Z     446. 		
2026-03-18T16:50:20.4173297Z     447. 		bool is_enum = peek() == "enum"_tok;
2026-03-18T16:50:20.4173889Z     448. 		bool is_struct = peek() == "struct"_tok || peek() == "class"_tok;
2026-03-18T16:50:20.4174467Z     449. 		
2026-03-18T16:50:20.4174878Z     450. 		// Look ahead to check if it's really an inline definition
2026-03-18T16:50:40.9993663Z 
2026-03-18T16:50:40.9994208Z function:
2026-03-18T16:50:40.9994581Z   name: edit
2026-03-18T16:50:40.9995577Z   args:
2026-03-18T16:50:40.9998694Z     new_str: |
2026-03-18T16:50:40.9999160Z       		// (re-get type_spec since we modified it above)
2026-03-18T16:50:41.0000061Z       		const TypeSpecifierNode& final_type_spec = type_result.node()->as<TypeSpecifierNode>();
2026-03-18T16:50:41.0000930Z       		
2026-03-18T16:50:41.0001249Z       		// Build qualified name if we're inside a struct.
2026-03-18T16:50:41.0001858Z       		// Walk struct_parsing_context_stack_ (which includes the current struct since it
2026-03-18T16:50:41.0002876Z       		// was pushed before member parsing begins) to build the full struct chain at any
2026-03-18T16:50:41.0004047Z       		// depth, then also register a namespace-prefixed version for ADL.
2026-03-18T16:50:41.0004784Z       		// This mirrors the registration done for direct enum declarations in
2026-03-18T16:50:41.0005960Z       		// parse_struct_declaration (the nested-enum block after addNestedEnumIndex()).
2026-03-18T16:50:41.0006622Z       		// We do NOT register the simple alias name (alias_name alone) when inside a struct:
2026-03-18T16:50:41.0007216Z       		// doing so would add the alias TypeInfo (no EnumTypeInfo) under the simple key,
2026-03-18T16:50:41.0008133Z       		// so unscoped-enum enumerator lookup in generateQualifiedIdentifierIr would find
2026-03-18T16:50:41.0008972Z       		// the alias TypeInfo instead of the original enum TypeInfo and crash.
2026-03-18T16:50:41.0009827Z       		// For non-struct aliases the simple name is correct (registered below).
2026-03-18T16:50:41.0010474Z       		StringHandle qualified_alias_name = alias_name;
2026-03-18T16:50:41.0010899Z       		if (struct_ref) {
2026-03-18T16:50:41.0011302Z       			NamespaceHandle current_ns = gSymbolTable.get_current_namespace_handle();
2026-03-18T16:50:41.0012032Z       			std::string_view current_ns_name = gNamespaceRegistry.getQualifiedName(current_ns);
2026-03-18T16:50:41.0012460Z       
2026-03-18T16:50:41.0012916Z       			// Build struct-chain-relative name (e.g., "Container::AliasStatus" for 1 level,
2026-03-18T16:50:41.0013501Z       			// "A::B::AliasStatus" for 2 levels of struct nesting)
2026-03-18T16:50:41.0013841Z       			StringBuilder chain_builder;
2026-03-18T16:50:41.0014261Z       			for (const auto& ctx : struct_parsing_context_stack_) {
2026-03-18T16:50:41.0014686Z       				chain_builder.append(ctx.struct_name).append("::");
2026-03-18T16:50:41.0015167Z       			}
2026-03-18T16:50:41.0015427Z       			chain_builder.append(alias_name);
2026-03-18T16:50:41.0016032Z       			StringHandle struct_relative_handle = StringTable::getOrInternStringHandle(chain_builder.commit());
2026-03-18T16:50:41.0016577Z       
2026-03-18T16:50:41.0017047Z       			// Always register the struct-chain-relative name (replaces old back()-based build)
2026-03-18T16:50:41.0017738Z       			TypeInfo& alias_info = register_type_alias(struct_relative_handle, final_type_spec);
2026-03-18T16:50:41.0018194Z       
2026-03-18T16:50:41.0018378Z       			if (!current_ns_name.empty()) {
2026-03-18T16:50:41.0018934Z       				// Also register namespace-qualified name "ns::Container::AliasStatus" so that

2026-03-18T16:50:41.0021786Z       				}

2026-03-18T16:50:41.0022150Z       			return ParseResult::success();
2026-03-18T16:50:41.0022399Z       		}
2026-03-18T16:50:41.0022561Z

2026-03-18T16:50:41.0023591Z

2026-03-18T16:53:16.5982012Z         lookups work from outside the namespace. Avoids registering the simple alias
2026-03-18T16:53:16.5982864Z         name to prevent unscoped-enum enumerator lookup crashes.

2026-03-18T16:53:16.5986308Z         so pointer identity is stable across copies and vector reallocations).
2026-03-18T16:53:16.5986956Z

2026-03-18T16:53:43.6229498Z 
2026-03-18T16:53:43.6231132Z copilot: All code changes are complete and validated (1579 pass / 0 fail). There is a merge conflict in `docs/MISSING_FEATURES.md` blocking the push. Here is a summary of what was implemented to address the unresolved Devin review comments:
2026-03-18T16:53:43.6232711Z 
2026-03-18T16:53:43.6232855Z **Changes made:**
2026-03-18T16:53:43.6233071Z 
2026-03-18T16:53:43.6235829Z 1. **`src/Parser_Decl_StructEnum.cpp`** — Walk the full `struct_parsing_context_stack_` (not just `back()`) to build the complete struct chain at any nesting depth. This fixes `ns::A::B::C` type registration for 2-level and 3-level nested structs in namespaces.
2026-03-18T16:53:43.6237382Z 
2026-03-18T16:53:43.6239867Z 2. **`src/Parser_Decl_TypedefUsing.cpp`** — Register both the struct-chain-relative name (`Container::AliasStatus`) and the namespace-qualified name (`ns::Container::AliasStatus`) for `using`/`typedef` aliases inside structs, mirroring what direct enum declarations do. Avoids registering the simple alias name to prevent enumerator lookup crashes.
2026-03-18T16:53:43.6242224Z 
2026-03-18T16:53:43.6243994Z 3. **`src/OverloadResolution.h`** — Added a stable-pointer fallback set (`existing_ptrs`) for operator ADL dedup when functions don't yet have a mangled name (ASTNode stores `T*` via `std::any`, so pointer identity is stable).
2026-03-18T16:53:43.6245751Z 
2026-03-18T16:53:43.6247277Z 4. **`src/IRConverter_ConvertMain.cpp`** — Replaced `assert(src_it != end)` with graceful error logging + early return to avoid crashes when an unresolved enum-alias enumerator is used as a variable initializer.
2026-03-18T16:53:43.6248681Z 
2026-03-18T16:53:43.6249295Z 5. **3 new tests**: `test_2level_ns_struct_ret0.cpp`, `test_3level_ns_struct_ret0.cpp`, `test_typedef_alias_adl_ret0.cpp`
2026-03-18T16:53:43.6250108Z 
2026-03-18T16:53:43.6250941Z 6. **`docs/MISSING_FEATURES.md`** + **`docs/KNOWN_ISSUES.md`**: Documented all fixes and the unscoped enum value access through aliases as a known limitation.
2026-03-18T16:53:43.6251953Z 
2026-03-18T16:53:43.6252876Z copilot-reply: 4083699079: Implemented all fixes from Devin's unresolved comments (commit 02f42f4, pending merge conflict resolution in docs/MISSING_FEATURES.md):
2026-03-18T16:53:43.6253994Z 
2026-03-18T16:53:43.6255984Z 1. **Deeply nested struct types (3+ levels)** — `parse_struct_declaration` now walks the full `struct_parsing_context_stack_` to build `ns::A::B::C` at any depth. New tests: `test_2level_ns_struct_ret0.cpp`, `test_3level_ns_struct_ret0.cpp`.
2026-03-18T16:53:43.6257454Z 
2026-03-18T16:53:43.6260729Z 2. **typedef/using alias qualified-name registration** — `parse_member_type_alias` now registers both the struct-chain-relative name and the namespace-qualified name (`ns::Container::AliasStatus`) in `gTypesByName`, mirroring direct enum registration. New test: `test_typedef_alias_adl_ret0.cpp`. Note: direct enum *value* access through unscoped aliases (`Container::AliasStatus::Ok`) is a pre-existing limitation, documented in `KNOWN_ISSUES.md`.
2026-03-18T16:53:43.6263501Z 
2026-03-18T16:53:43.6264618Z 3. **No-mangled-name ADL dedup** — Added stable-pointer fallback set in `findBinaryOperatorOverloadWithFreeFunction` for candidates without mangled names.
2026-03-18T16:53:43.6265918Z 
2026-03-18T16:53:43.6266988Z 4. **Assertion crash fix** — Replaced `assert()` with graceful error+return in `handleVariableDecl` when a variable initializer name is not found in scope.
2026-03-18T16:53:43.6268302Z 
2026-03-18T16:53:43.6268493Z Suite: 1579 pass / 0 fail / 56 expected-fail.
2026-03-18T16:53:43.6268837Z

2026-03-18T16:53:43.6276067Z
